title: Mishimoto Intercooler for Ford Powerstroke 2003-2007 6.0L Silver
description: Whether your truck is decked out with engine modifications or it's bone stock, adding a Mishimoto intercooler will improve performance and extend engine life. This Mishimoto intercooler has a direct fit design for the 2003 to 2007 Ford Super Duty pickup trucks equipped with the 6.0L PowerStroke. This performance intercooler is manufactured with heavy-duty cast aluminum end tanks and a stout bar-and-plate core, which is designed to decrease intake temperatures, improve airflow and reduce pressure drop. This pressure drop reduction not only extends the life of the turbocharger, but it also improves the overall efficiency of the engine. The Mishimoto intercooler provides a step up from a factory intercooler with a 32.5 percent increase in core volume resulting in numerous performance benefits including increased pressure tolerance and lower EGTs. The Mishimoto intercooler has a 3.35-inch bar-and-plate core is, which is 33 percent thicker than factory intercooler, with end tanks that are strategically designed for optimal airflow and lower intake air temperatures. The more air you can add to your engine and the cooler that air is, the better your engine will perform. This Mishimoto intercooler is covered by a Lifetime Warranty.

title: Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration System for 2003-2007 Ford Powerstroke 6.0L
description: .two-column { display:flex; flex-direction:row; } @media only screen and (max-width: 600px) { .two-column, .two-column.reverse { flex-direction:column; } } .two-column > div { flex:1; margin:10px; max-width: 700px } Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration Kit – 6.0L Powerstroke (Updated Design) Fits 2003–2007 Ford F-250, F-350, F-450 & Excursion Key Features • Updated Filter Head Design – Replaces the retired “tombstone” style with our latest, more reliable filter head • One-Piece Molded Hose – Eliminates failure points found in multi-piece hose setups • Upgraded Internal Hose Connections – Engineered for a secure, leak-free seal and long-term durability • Genuine WIX Coolant Filter – Proven filtration performance with easy spin-on serviceability • Factory-Style Mounting – Utilizes existing mounting locations with no cutting or drilling required • Bypass Filtration System – Continuously filters coolant without restricting flow • Extends Component Life – Helps protect oil cooler, EGR cooler, and water pump from debris damage • 1-Year Warranty – Backed for reliability and peace of mind Product Overview The Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration Kit for the 6.0 Powerstroke has been fully updated—delivering improved reliability, cleaner installation, and long-term durability. This is the same proven system trusted by thousands of 6.0 owners, now upgraded with our latest design improvements. The original “tombstone” style filter head has officially been retired and is no longer available. In its place is our new filter head design, built for better strength, consistency, and overall performance. The hose system has also been completely refined with a one-piece molded design and upgraded internal connections. These improvements eliminate common weak points found in older multi-piece setups and provide a more secure, dependable system built to last. The 6.0 Powerstroke is known for coolant contamination—casting sand, rust, and debris circulating through the system from the factory and over time. This kit continuously filters those contaminants out while the engine runs, helping prevent oil cooler blockage, EGR cooler failure, and premature water pump wear. Installation remains straightforward with a factory-style mounting bracket and no cutting or drilling required. Maintenance is simple with an easy spin-on WIX filter. This isn’t just a replacement—it’s a direct evolution of the original, built to perform better in every way. Recommended Filter Replacement Schedule • First filter: 500 miles • Second filter: 1,000 miles • Third filter: 3,000 miles • After that: replace with every oil filter service What’s Included (1) Updated Sinister Diesel Filter Head (1) Genuine WIX Coolant Filter (1) One-Piece Molded Hose Assembly (1) Mounting Bracket All Required Installation Hardware Why Choose Sinister Diesel Since 2009, Sinister Diesel has led the industry in coolant filtration for the 6.0 Powerstroke. We don’t just maintain our products—we continuously improve them. This updated kit reflects that commitment, delivering a stronger, cleaner, and more refined solution to one of the 6.0’s most common failure points. FAQ What does this kit do for my 6.0 Powerstroke? It filters out casting sand, rust, and metal debris from your coolant. Removing these contaminants helps prevent oil cooler and EGR cooler failures while protecting your water pump and maintaining proper coolant flow. Why is it a bypass system instead of inline? A bypass system filters a portion of coolant at a time while the engine runs, eventually cleaning the entire system without restricting flow. Inline filters can become a restriction if clogged, but a bypass design keeps coolant moving safely at all times. What filter is included and does it affect coolant chemistry? This kit includes a genuine WIX 24070 coolant filter. It’s a high-flow, non-chemical filter that does not alter coolant chemistry and is safe for use with ELC or standard coolant. How often should the filter be replaced? • First filter: 500 miles • Second filter: 1,000 miles • Third filter: 3,000 miles • After that: replace with every oil filter service How long does installation take and what’s involved? Installation typically takes around 2 hours using basic hand tools. The kit mounts to factory locations with no cutting or drilling required. What’s included in the kit? The kit includes the updated Sinister Diesel filter head, one-piece molded hose assembly, mounting bracket, genuine WIX coolant filter, and all required installation hardware.

Even a new engine can circulate leftover casting sand, rust, and metal flakes through the coolant system—slowly clogging your oil cooler, heater core, and water pump. Over time, that contamination leads to overheating and expensive failures. This kit solves the problem by adding a bypass-style filtration system that continuously cleans your coolant as the engine runs. The CNC-machined 6061 billet aluminum filter block mounts directly to existing factory holes for a clean, no-drill installation. It’s anodized in Sinister Blue for corrosion protection and durability. Each kit includes a genuine WIX 24070 filter, known for its efficiency and reliability, along with pre-assembled multi-layer silicone hoses featuring crimped brass fittings for a professional, leak-free connection. Installation is simple—most users can complete it in under two hours with basic hand tools. Once installed, you’ll enjoy cleaner coolant, lower maintenance costs, and extended engine life. Recommended Filter Replacement Schedule First filter: 500 miles Second filter: 1,000 miles Third filter: 3,000 miles After that: every oil filter service Kit Includes CNC-Machined Billet Aluminum Filter Block (Anodized in Sinister Blue) Genuine WIX 24070 Coolant Filter Factory-Style Mounting Bracket Pre-Assembled Silicone Hoses with Brass Crimp Fittings All Fittings and Installation Hardware Why Buy From Sinister Diesel Since 2009, Sinister Diesel has been the leading name in Powerstroke reliability upgrades. Our coolant filtration kits are precision-built in the USA and trusted by diesel enthusiasts nationwide. Each kit is designed, tested, and backed by Sinister’s proven commitment to performance and quality—protecting your engine and giving your truck the reliability it deserves. Q&A What does this kit do? It filters casting sand, rust, and metal shavings from your cooling system to protect the oil cooler, heater core, and water pump. Cleaner coolant keeps your 7.3 running cooler and more efficiently. Why is it a bypass filter instead of inline? The bypass system filters a small percentage of coolant at a time until all of it has passed through the filter. Inline systems can block flow if the filter clogs. With the Sinister Diesel bypass design, coolant continues to circulate even if the filter becomes full—no risk of overheating or engine damage. What filter is included? A genuine WIX 24070 spin-on coolant filter is included. It’s proven for high-flow, low-restriction performance and uses a standard 11/16-16 thread for easy replacement. Does it change coolant chemistry? No. The WIX filter is purely mechanical—it doesn’t add or remove any chemical additives and works perfectly with Extended Life Coolant (ELC). What materials are used? The filter block is CNC-machined from 6061 billet aluminum and anodized in Sinister Blue. The hoses are multi-layer high-temp silicone with pre-crimped, leak-proof fittings. Is installation easy? Yes. The mounting bracket uses existing factory holes—no drilling or cutting required. Everything you need for installation is included. How long does installation take? About 2 hours on average, depending on experience. How often should the filter be replaced? • First filter: 500 miles • Second filter: 1,000 miles • Third filter: 3,000 miles • Then change with every oil filter service What’s included in the kit? • Billet aluminum filter bloc

title: Sinister Diesel EGR Cooler for 2004-2007 Ford Powerstroke 6.0L
description: Sinister Diesel EGR Cooler – 2004–2007 Ford F-250 / F-350 / Excursion 6.0L Powerstroke 50-State Legal CARB EO# D-721 Fitment Fits 2004–2007 Ford F-250, F-350 Super Duty, and Ford Excursion equipped with the 6.0L Powerstroke Diesel engine. Key Features Stainless Steel Construction – Built for long-term durability and reliability High-Flow Internal Design – Improves coolant and exhaust gas flow compared to restrictive factory cores Fully TIG Welded Construction – Eliminates the failure-prone brazed joints found in factory coolers Pressure Tested – Verified for leak-free performance before leaving our facility Direct Replacement Design – Installs in the factory location using factory connections 50-State Legal – CARB EO# D-721 approved Compatible with Stock and Tuned Trucks – Works with factory calibrations and aftermarket tuning 2-Year Warranty – Backed by Sinister Diesel for quality and reliability Product Overview The factory 6.0L Powerstroke EGR cooler is one of the most common failure points on the engine. Over time, the factory cooler's internal brazed joints can crack and fail, allowing coolant to enter the exhaust stream and intake system. When this occurs, coolant can make its way into the combustion chamber, potentially leading to white smoke, coolant loss, overheating concerns, head gasket failure, or even catastrophic engine damage. Repair costs can quickly escalate into thousands of dollars. The Sinister Diesel EGR Cooler was engineered to eliminate the weaknesses of the factory design while maintaining full EGR functionality and emissions compliance. Instead of using restrictive cooling fins and brazed internal connections, our cooler utilizes high-flow stainless steel tubing and fully TIG welded construction for significantly improved durability and reliability. The increased flow capacity helps reduce restriction while providing a stronger design capable of withstanding the demands of towing, daily driving, and performance applications. Every Sinister Diesel EGR Cooler is extensively tested to ensure proper fitment and long-term reliability. As a direct replacement for the factory unit, it installs using factory mounting locations and connections while retaining full compatibility with both stock and tuned vehicles. Best of all, this EGR cooler is fully CARB compliant under EO# D-721, making it legal for use in all 50 states. What’s Included (1) Sinister Diesel EGR Cooler Why Choose Sinister Diesel Since 2009, Sinister Diesel has been developing solutions for the most common 6.0L Powerstroke failures. Our EGR coolers are engineered to provide superior durability, improved flow, and long-term reliability while maintaining full emissions compliance and 50-state legality.

title: Sinister Diesel 58v FICM for 2003-2007 Ford Powerstroke 6.0L
description: .two-column { display:flex; flex-direction:row; } @media only screen and (max-width: 600px) { .two-column, .two-column.reverse { flex-direction:column; } } .two-column > div { flex:1; margin:10px; max-width: 700px } Sinister Diesel Remanufactured 58V FICM – 2003–2007 Ford 6.0L Powerstroke ⚠️ Built to Order – Please Read Before Ordering Every Sinister Diesel 58V FICM is built, upgraded, and extensively tested to order. Due to the time required for remanufacturing, calibration, and quality control testing, please allow 2–3 weeks before your order ships. Orders are processed on a first-come, first-served basis and cannot be expedited through production. Not for Sale in California Fitment Fits 2003–2007 Ford F-250, F-350, F-450, F-550 Super Duty and Ford Excursion equipped with the 6.0L Powerstroke Diesel engine. Key Features 58-Volt Output – Increased injector voltage for improved performance and reliability Improved Cold Starts – Faster injector activation during cold weather operation Improved Injector Response Time – More consistent injector operation and throttle response Reduced Injector Stiction – Helps combat one of the most common 6.0L injector issues Updated 4-Pin Hardware Design – Every Sinister Diesel 58V FICM is built using the newer 4-pin architecture for improved reliability and long-term durability Upgraded Internal Components – Designed to address common factory FICM failures Latest Software Calibration – Includes inductive injector heating strategy for cold-weather operation Fully Tested Prior to Shipment – Every unit undergoes extensive electrical and functional testing Plug-and-Play Installation – Compatible with both 4-pin and 7-pin factory applications 2-Year Warranty – Backed by Sinister Diesel for quality and reliability Product Overview The Sinister Diesel Remanufactured 58V FICM is designed to improve both the reliability and performance of the 6.0L Powerstroke fuel injection system. The factory Fuel Injection Control Module (FICM) operates at approximately 48 volts and is known for developing internal component failures over time due to heat cycling, vibration, and age. As voltage output decreases, injector performance suffers, often resulting in hard starts, rough idle, poor throttle response, injector stiction, and reduced engine performance. Our upgraded 58V FICM is engineered to address these issues by increasing injector operating voltage while upgrading critical internal circuitry and driver components that are exposed to the additional voltage demand. We also correct many of the original design weaknesses found in factory FICMs, creating a more durable and reliable unit. The increased voltage helps improve injector response time, reduce stiction-related concerns, and provide faster cold-weather starts. The latest software calibration also includes an inductive heating strategy that activates during cold engine starts, helping warm the injector spool valves and further reduce stiction during cold-weather operation. Every Sinister Diesel 58V FICM is subjected to extensive testing before shipment. Units must successfully pass full power supply load testing, injector driver verification, PCM communication testing, waveform analysis, self-diagnostic testing, and complete scan-tool validation before being approved for sale. Why We Don't Use "Performance Tuned" FICM Calibrations Many 6.0L Powerstroke owners ask about performance-tuned FICMs that claim to increase horsepower through modified injector timing strategies. While these calibrations may offer small power gains, they can also introduce additional variables into the injection system that may affect drivability, diagnostics, long-term reliability, and injector life. The Sinister Diesel 58-Volt FICM takes a different approach. Rather than altering Ford's proven injector control strategy, our FICM retains the factory calibration while increasing injector voltage from the stock 48 volts to a more robust 58 volts. This allows the injectors to react faster and more consistently, improving cold starts, throttle response, injector operation, and overall drivability without changing the factory programming your engine was designed around. By maintaining the factory calibration, the Sinister Diesel 58-Volt FICM delivers OEM-like compatibility with stock and aftermarket PCM tuning while providing the benefits of a stronger, more stable injector control system. There are no aggressive timing changes, no specialty performance files to manage, and no concerns about compatibility between the FICM and your engine tune. For Powerstroke owners looking for the best combination of reliability, drivability, and performance, we believe a stock-calibrated 58-Volt FICM is the smartest long-term solution. To check compatibility, inspect the throttle valve located between the factory intake elbow and the intake manifold. Key Features • Aluminum construction for strength and heat resistance • Fully TIG-welded, mandrel-bent tubing for smooth, unrestricted airflow • CNC-machined billet aluminum flanges for precise, leak-free fitment • Direct bolt-on replacement using factory mounting points • Designed to increase airflow for improved throttle response and performance • Durable Sinister Blue powder coat finish for corrosion resistance and clean appearance • Backed by a 2-year Sinister Diesel warranty Product Overview The Sinister Diesel Intake Elbow is designed to improve airflow efficiency and unlock additional performance from your 6.0L Powerstroke. By replacing the restrictive factory elbow with a high-flow aluminum design, this upgrade allows your engine to breathe more freely, resulting in improved throttle response, smoother acceleration, and increased overall efficiency. Constructed from mandrel-bent aluminum and fully TIG-welded for durability, this intake elbow is built to perform under towing, daily driving, and demanding conditions. CNC-machined billet flanges ensure a precise seal at the intake manifold, while the signature Sinister Blue powder coat adds both protection and a refined, professional look to your engine bay. Installation is straightforward with no cutting or fabrication required, making this a true bolt-on upgrade that delivers both performance and reliability. What’s Included • Sinister Diesel aluminum intake elbow with Sinister Blue powder coat finish • Complete installation hardware Frequently Asked Questions Q: Is this intake elbow street legal? A: Yes. This intake elbow is 50-state legal and CARB certified under EO# D-721-6 for 2005–2007 Ford F-250 / F-350 and 2005 Excursion models equipped with the 6.0L Powerstroke. Q: Will this fit E-Series vans or Cab & Chassis trucks? A: No. Due to differences in engine bay layout and component clearance, this intake elbow is not compatible with E-Series or Cab & Chassis configurations. Q: How do I know if my 2003–2004 Powerstroke is compatible? A: Inspect the throttle valve located between the stock intake elbow and the intake manifold. If the valve has electrical wires connected, it is an active throttle valve and this intake elbow will not fit. If there are no wires, it is a non-active valve and this intake elbow installs without issue. Q: What kind of performance improvements can I expect? A: Most users report improved throttle response and smoother acceleration due to increased airflow and reduced restriction. Q: Is tuning required after installation? A: No. This intake elbow is designed to work with stock tuning and does not require any calibration changes. Q: What finish does the intake elbow have? A: Each intake elbow is finished in Sinister Blue powder coat for durability, corrosion resistance, and a premium under-hood appearance. Q: How long does installation take? title: ARP Head Stud Kit for 2001-2015 Duramax (ARP2000)
description: For all ARP Stud Purchases, Billing and Shipping addresses must match. ***All Sales are final for ARP Head Stud Kits, No Returns Signature Required upon Delivery To prevent expensive cylinder head damage and head gasket failure, be sure to add ARP head studs when upgrading or rebuilding the Duramax diesel V-8 in your 2001 to 2015 Chevrolet Silverado and GMC Sierra heavy-duty trucks. ARP head studs are a favorite among professional diesel engine builders with a design that offers superior tensile strength and reliability compared to factory head bolts. Unlike the factory head bolts, ARP head studs have greater strength and are less likely to fail. ARP uses a premium grade 8740 alloy and a proprietary ARP2000 alloy, and heat treats the head studs to high-psi to ensure that these studs won’t stretch, bend or break under the high pressure delivered by today’s high-output diesel engines. title: FASS Titanium Signature Series Diesel Fuel Lift Pump for 2005-2018 Dodge Cummins 5.9L/6.7L (100gph)
description: All FASS Product Sales are Final - Non Refundable FASS Whisper Technology (FWT) was engineered to meet stringent demands of today's quiet diesel engines. FASS Whisper Technology was in development and tested for over two and a half years to meet FASS Fuel System's high quality and durability standards. FASS Whisper Technology effectively and drastically reduces the sound levels of the Signature Series FASS Diesel Fuel System to a "whisper" while at the same time increases fuel flow rates. Performance Radius Cuts (PRC) are an advanced machining technique used to improve the flow of fuel through the FASS System by creating smooth flowing cuts within the aluminum housing. This prevents the reintroduction of air and vapor back into the fuel and to prevent eddy currents from forming within the corner of a 90-degree sharp angle. FASS Titanium Signature Fuel Systems are the only Fuel Air Separation System that incorporates these exclusive Performance Radius Cuts, lesser competitors commonly use T-Bone and square 90-degree fittings that can limit fuel flow, causing restriction and reintroducing air back into your fuel. Mass Flow Return (MFR) technology is used to continually polish your fuel by keeping a positive pressure on the fuel being delivered to the engine to prevent cavitation along with galling and scoring. Then returning the fuel, that's not yet needed, back to the tank after it has been passed through the filtration process, removing dirt, air vapor and water. This means your fuel is being constantly "Polished" (cleaned), guaranteeing that your diesel truck always has the cleanest fuel possible. The MFR in combination with the optional FASS Fuel Heater options will help insure extreme cold weather operations! Bolt On Bracket Mounts old mounting styles required drilling or welding then mounting your new pump. This has now been simplified with our new bolt on brackets that secure your truck bed to your frame. Mounting your Titanium Signature Series system takes only minutes and all the brackets are made from machined steel for superior support and strength. Features: New Bolt On Bracket Installation Includes Hardware/Install Kit Preset 8-10 PSI Fuel Pressure Complete Replacement Pump 100 Gallons Per Hour Flow Rating Removes Water, Air and Debris from Diesel Fuel 2 Micron Filtration Rating Protects and Extends the life of your injectors Improved Cold Weather Starts Decreases Carbon Monoxide and Carbon Dioxide Emissions 1/2 Inch Fuel Lines and Ports Fuel Mileage Improvement Improved Fuel Volume Delivery Smoother Engine Idle Optional Electric Heater Ports Optional Fuel Pressure Gauge Port Limited Lifetime Warranty 30,000 Mile Filter Life Expectancy Quietest Motor On The Market

title: SCT Livewire TS Performance Programmer and Monitor for Ford
description: All Tuners & Programmers Sales are Final - Non Refundable Not for sale in California! Billing and Shipping Address must match for this order to be processed This product is not compatible with trucks where emissions equipment has been removed To unlock the maximum performance potential of your diesel or gasoline engine, the SCT Livewire TS is an easy-to-use programmer that can recalibrate your Ford Powerstroke engine management system to deliver maximum horsepower and torque, increased throttle response, firmer shifts and even improved fuel economy. The SCT Livewire TS has a suction-mount four-inch full-color touch screen display, pre-loaded with dyno-proven performance tune files to optimize air/fuel ratio, ignition timing, fuel curves and peak boost. Beyond those tune profiles, it can hold up to 10 custom tunes. Aside from these user-adjustable tune options, the SCT Livewire TS also lets users set and adjust warnings and alerts as well as select screen graphics and gauge layouts. The SCT Livewire TS even offers a Switch On-The-Fly feature allowing the programmer to easily switch between tunes without having to wait for a reset or reprogram. This means that you can be towing with your truck one minute and drag racing on the track the next. The display comes on and off automatically, and there's even a sleep mode to ensure that the programmer doesn't cause a parasitic electrical draw that might kill your battery. In addition to being able to log and monitor high-speed data, the SCT Livewire TS can also be used as a scan tool to read and clear DTCs such as check engine lights. SCT Livewire TS Features: Color 4 inch Touch Screen Display Rear View Camera Input Automatic On / Off / Sleep Mode Holds up to 10 Custom Tunes Reads & Clears DTC Codes Built-In Performance Calculator High Speed Data Logging & Monitoring User Adjustable Tune Options User Selectable Graphics & Gauge Layouts Available for both Gas & Diesel Vehicles User Adjustable Warning / Alerts Windshield Mount Included Emissions Compliance Disclaimer: This tuner is designed and intended solely for use on vehicles equipped with fully functional emissions control systems, including but not limited to DPF, EGR, and catalytic converters. It is NOT intended for use on vehicles where any emissions equipment has been removed, disabled, or otherwise tampered with. Use of this product on emissions-deleted vehicles is strictly prohibited and may violate federal, state, or local laws. title: Edge Products EAS Turbo Timer
description: Edge Products EAS Turbo Timer The EAS Universal Turbo Timer part # 98604 is designed for all diesel engines besides the 2006-2011 Dodge Cummins. The 2006-2011 Dodge Cummins EAS Turbo timer part # is 98612. The 2006-2011 Dodge Cummins uses different connectors for installation. A turbo timer is a device designed to keep an engine running for a pre-specified period of time or a until a specific temperature is reached in order to automatically execute the cool-down period required to prevent premature turbo wear and failure. This also allows the lubricating oil from the engine to circulate properly so the turbine won't burn the lubricating oil that would otherwise be trapped within the charger with the turbine rotating at high speed. The Edge Accessory System (EAS) was created with future expandability in mind. The architecture of the EAS allows users to connect multiple accessories, daisy-chain style, to the CS and CTS platform. The ability to daisy-chain multiple accessories using the EAS allows users to monitor and display dozens of parameters on a single device. *Turbo timers are only available to work with the CS and CTS product lines. In order to use the turbo timer you must have an EAS Starter kit (part # 98602). EAS Starter kits come with all Juice w/ Attitude CS and CTS products and the EAS Starter Kit w/ Expandable EGT (part # 98603).

title: Edge Products EAS Pressure Sensor
description: Edge Products EAS PRESSURE SENSOR Edge's EAS Pressure Sensor is a 0 - 100 PSIg 1/8" NPT sensor that plugs directly into the Universal Sensor Input system (p/n 98605). Edge's Pressure Sensor comes equipped with the proper wires and connectors to plug right into the Universal Sensor Input system saving time and money on cutting, splicing, and crimping wire terminals to connect the sensor. Use Edge's Pressure Sensor to monitor data such as Boost PSI, Fuel Rail Pressure, Air Bag Pressure, Lift Pump Pressure, Pre and Post Intercooler Pressure and many more. Tolerance: +-1.5% Full Span over temperature range of -20C to 85C and +-2.4% Full Span over temperature range of -40C to 135C. Note: Requires EAS Starter Kit in order to operate

title: Garrett Power Max Ball-bearing Turbo Kit for Ford Powerstroke 1999.5-2003 7.3L
description: The Garrett 739619-5004S PowerMax GTP38R Turbocharger is designed to fit the 7.3 Powerstroke diesel engine in 1999.5-2003 Ford F-250, F-350, and Excursion trucks. With the Garrett exclusive Ball Bearing Cartridges, the GTP38R provide unbeatable response, efficiency and durability at elevated boost levels for the owner that wants to aggressive performance from their vehicle. In addition, the Powermax turbo mounts to the factory pedestal and retains the use of OE intake and exhaust systems for easy drop-in installation. The GTP38R PowerMax features an 88mm compressor wheel that is 10% bigger and provides 35% more flow than the compressor wheel found on your stock turbo. Also included is a ported shroud housing that improves compressor flow range and surge control. The included 1.00 A/R housing allows for free flowing exhaust with reduced back pressure and can even reduce exhaust gas temperatures up to 200 degrees. Due to increased intake volume, we highly recommend fueling upgrades including injectors and tuning. Features: Garrett Ball Bearing cartridges Drop-In Installation 88mm Compressor wheel 1.00 A/R Turbine Housing Ported Shroud Housing Kit Includes Garrett GTP38R Turbocharger Hose Band Clamp Oil supply hardware

title: Sinister Diesel Heavy Duty Oil Cooler for 2003-2007 Ford Powerstroke 6.0L
description: Sinister Diesel Oil Cooler for 6.0 Powerstroke - Product Overview The Sinister Diesel Oil Cooler for the 6.0 Powerstroke engine is engineered to replace the factory oil cooler, which is prone to failure. Designed as a direct drop-in replacement, this upgraded oil cooler improves the cooling efficiency of your engine oil, helping to extend engine life and prevent costly repairs. Constructed from high-quality aluminum, it offers greater durability and resistance to corrosion compared to the factory model, ensuring long-lasting performance. The larger surface area and improved flow design enhance the cooling capacity, reducing the risk of clogs and increasing overall engine reliability. This oil cooler comes complete with new O-rings and gaskets, ensuring a leak-free installation without the need for any modifications to your engine or cooling system. Key Features and Benefits Meets or Exceeds OEM Standards – Precision-manufactured to match or surpass factory specifications. Direct Drop-In Replacement – Easy to install with no modifications required. Better Flow Rate than OEM – Larger passages for improved flow and reduced clogging risk. High-Quality Aluminum Construction – More durable and corrosion-resistant than the factory oil cooler. 2 Year Warranty – Backed by Sinister Diesel’s commitment to quality and performance. Why Upgrade to the Sinister Diesel Oil Cooler? The best oil cooler for a 6.0 Powerstroke is one that improves reliability and flow without introducing new failure points—and that’s exactly what the Sinister Diesel Oil Cooler is designed to do. Since 2010, Sinister Diesel has engineered its oil cooler as a direct-fit replacement for the factory unit, maintaining the OEM location while significantly improving internal flow and durability. Each cooler is individually leak-tested before shipping to ensure quality and reliability right out of the box. Unlike restrictive factory designs, the Sinister Diesel oil cooler uses higher-grade internal materials and optimized passage sizing to promote more consistent oil flow and improved heat transfer. This helps reduce oil temperature issues common in the 6.0 platform and supports long-term engine health. Some aftermarket oil cooler systems relocate the cooler outside of the engine. While that may sound beneficial, it introduces added complexity, longer oil routing, increased install time, and more potential failure points such as hoses, fittings, and exposure to road debris. It can also impact oil flow during cold starts due to the increased system volume and distance. By keeping the cooler in the factory location and improving the internal design, Sinister Diesel provides a solution that enhances performance and reliability without unnecessary risk or installation complexity. If you’re looking for a proven, efficient, and dependable upgrade, the Sinister Diesel Oil Cooler remains one of the most trusted options available for the 6.0 Powerstroke. Kit Includes: (1) Aluminum Oil Cooler Complete Gasket & O-Ring Kit Large Black Gasket (left side, irregular shape) This is the oil cooler housing to engine block gasket . It seals the oil cooler assembly to the block. Round Black O-Ring (center, single circle) This is the oil cooler center section O-ring (fits between cooler and housing). Large Blue Gasket (right side, irregular shape with multiple openings) This is the oil cooler housing cover gasket (sometimes called the oil cooler top cover or filter header gasket). It seals the oil cooler cover to the housing. Multiple Small Black O-Rings (sitting inside the blue gasket) These seal various oil passages inside the cooler housing (for high-pressure oil feed, return, and filter bypass passages). Rectangular Black Rubber with Screen (upper right) This is the oil cooler screen / HPOP feed screen . It sits under the cooler cover and prevents debris from reaching the high-pressure oil pump (HPOP). Sinister Oil Cooler (center, upgraded aluminum stack) This is the replacement cooler itself (upgraded stacked-plate design). Product Compatibility This oil cooler is compatible with Ford Powerstroke 6.0 engines, offering a direct replacement for the factory oil cooler.

title: Replacement CAT Coolant Filter for Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration Kits
description: The Sinister Diesel CAT Coolant Replacement Kit fits all Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration Kits as well as all of our Bypass Oil/Coolant Filtration Kits and is available on many of them as an upgrade option. These replacement CAT coolant filters are designed to withstand the toughest conditions and are perfect for application in your Powerstroke, Duramax, or Cummins powered diesel truck. The advanced filtration in every CAT coolant filter removes contaminants like casting sand leftover from the manufacturing process, metal shavings from wear, rust, and mineral deposits in coolant that can reduce the effectiveness of both the cooling and lubrication system and lead to breakdowns, overheating, and expensive engine damage. On new installations of a Sinister Diesel CAT Coolant Filter, we recommend replacement of the first filter at 500 miles and replacement of the second and third filters after 3,000 miles each. After three filter changes, replacement can be done once a year. Features: Direct fit for our Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration Kits Advanced filtration to removed sands, mineral deposits, and metal shavings Includes: (1) CAT 435-5142 Coolant filter Q: What coolant filters work best with Sinister Diesel coolant filtration kits? A: The two most popular and proven options are the WIX 24070 and the CAT 435-5142. Both deliver excellent protection against casting sand, rust, and debris that can clog your heater core, EGR cooler, Oil Cooler or water pump. Q: What’s the difference between the WIX 24070 and the CAT 435-5142? A: The WIX 24070 is slightly taller at 5.25 inches, while the CAT 435-5142 is more compact at 4⅜ inches. The WIX filter is lighter (about 1 lb) and designed for smooth, high-flow operation, while the CAT filter is heavier (about 2 lb) with a thicker steel housing for extreme durability. Q: Which filter do most customers use? A: The WIX 24070 is our standard recommendation for most Sinister Diesel coolant filtration kits. It’s affordable, widely available, and requires no additives—just pure debris filtration and easy maintenance. Q: When should I consider using the CAT 435-5142 instead? A: The CAT 435-5142 is a great upgrade for heavy-duty or high-vibration applications. If your truck tows frequently, runs in harsh conditions, or you prefer OEM-grade construction, the CAT filter’s heavier shell and reinforced core are a solid choice. Q: Does either filter add coolant additives or chemicals? A: No—both filters are non-pelleted. That means they don’t release Supplemental Coolant Additives (SCAs). If you’re running Extended Life Coolant (ELC), that’s perfect—no extra maintenance needed. Q: Are both filters compatible with Sinister Diesel kits? A: Absolutely. Both the WIX 24070 and CAT 435-5142 thread directly into Sinister Diesel coolant filtration heads (11/16-16 thread). Fitment is perfect on Powerstroke, Cummins, and Duramax applications. Q: Which one is better for a limited space under the hood? A: The CAT 435-5142 is shorter at 4⅜", so it’s ideal if your installation area has less vertical clearance. (Which is why it's the only choice for some kits) Q: Which filter provides better coolant flow? A: The WIX 24070 typically offers slightly higher flow due to its taller design and lighter internal construction, making it ideal for daily drivers. Q: How often should these filters be replaced? A: Replace the first filter after 500 miles to remove initial debris from the system. Then replace the second filter around 1,000 miles, and the third one at 3,000 miles. After that, change the coolant filter with every oil filter service to keep your cooling system performing at its best. These intervals apply to both the WIX 24070 and CAT 435-5142. Q: What’s the bottom line better filter? WIX 24070: Best all-around filter for most trucks — easy, reliable, and affordable. (Most sold filter for Sinister Diesel Kits) CAT 435-5142: Heavy-duty option for extreme use or customers who prefer CAT’s OEM-grade toughness. Both keep your cooling system clean, protected, and performing at its best.

title: Ford Parts HPOP (High Pressure Oil Pump) for 2005-2007 Ford Powerstroke 6.0L
description: Sinister Diesel Remanufactured HPOP – 2005–2007 6.0L Powerstroke Key Features • Remanufactured OEM HPOP – Rebuilt from original Ford high pressure oil pumps, not aftermarket castings • Restored to OEM Specs – Meets factory performance and pressure standards • Fixes Common 6.0 Failure Point – Addresses internal seal leaks and premature pump failure • Restores Injection Pressure – Delivers proper oil pressure for consistent injector operation • Direct Drop-In Replacement – No modifications required for install • Reliable Performance – Ideal for failed or high-mileage HPOP replacement Product Overview The high pressure oil pump (HPOP) is one of the most common failure points on the 6.0L Powerstroke—and it’s not a matter of if, it’s when. Over time, internal seals wear out, pressure drops, and the pump can fail completely—leading to long crank, hard starts, or a no-start condition. All remanufactured HPOPs start as original Ford units, and this pump is rebuilt by a Ford-contracted supplier to meet OEM specifications. This ensures proper oil pressure delivery to your injectors—critical for engine performance and reliability. A properly functioning HPOP should produce 3,000–4,000 PSI at wide open throttle and 600–700 PSI at idle. When pressure drops below 500 PSI, injectors will not fire correctly—leaving your truck unable to start or run. Replacing a weak or failing HPOP before total failure helps prevent being stranded and avoids further issues in your high pressure oil system. Designed as a direct replacement for 2005–2007 6.0L Powerstroke engines, this unit installs without modification—making it a straightforward solution whether you're fixing a failure or proactively replacing a high-mileage pump. What’s Included (1) Ford Remanufactured HPOP Why Choose Sinister Diesel Since 2009, Sinister Diesel has focused on solving real-world failure points in diesel engines. The factory Fuel Injection Control Module (FICM) operates at approximately 48 volts and is known for developing internal component failures over time due to heat cycling, vibration, and age. As voltage output decreases, injector performance suffers, often resulting in hard starts, rough idle, poor throttle response, injector stiction, and reduced engine performance. Our upgraded 58V FICM is engineered to address these issues by increasing injector operating voltage while upgrading critical internal circuitry and driver components that are exposed to the additional voltage demand. We also correct many of the original design weaknesses found in factory FICMs, creating a more durable and reliable unit. The increased voltage helps improve injector response time, reduce stiction-related concerns, and provide faster cold-weather starts. The latest software calibration also includes an inductive heating strategy that activates during cold engine starts, helping warm the injector spool valves and further reduce stiction during cold-weather operation. Every Sinister Diesel 58V FICM is subjected to extensive testing before shipment. Units must successfully pass full power supply load testing, injector driver verification, PCM communication testing, waveform analysis, self-diagnostic testing, and complete scan-tool validation before being approved for sale. While these calibrations may offer small power gains, they can also introduce additional variables into the injection system that may affect drivability, diagnostics, long-term reliability, and injector life. The Sinister Diesel 58-Volt FICM takes a different approach. Rather than altering Ford's proven injector control strategy, our FICM retains the factory calibration while increasing injector voltage from the stock 48 volts to a more robust 58 volts. This allows the injectors to react faster and more consistently, improving cold starts, throttle response, injector operation, and overall drivability without changing the factory programming your engine was designed around. By maintaining the factory calibration, the Sinister Diesel 58-Volt FICM delivers OEM-like compatibility with stock and aftermarket PCM tuning while providing the benefits of a stronger, more stable injector control system. There are no aggressive timing changes, no specialty performance files to manage, and no concerns about compatibility between the FICM and your engine tune. title: Sinister Diesel Replacement Coolant Filter for Filtration Kits (WIX)
description: More than half of all catastrophic, premature diesel engine failures are related to improper cooling system maintenance techniques and habits. If your diesel truck utilizes a coolant filter, than look to WIX for proper replacement filters. This spin-on coolant filter uses a cellulose media to ensure optimal filtration. Don't wait until it's too late to replace your diesel truck's coolant filter. WIX coolant filter p/n 24070 has an 11/16-16 thread size, and its measurements include 5.214 inches in height and 3.694 inches for the outer diameter at the top. Q: What coolant filters work best with Sinister Diesel coolant filtration kits? A: The two most popular and proven options are the WIX 24070 and the CAT 435-5142. Both deliver excellent protection against casting sand, rust, and debris that can clog your heater core, EGR cooler, Oil Cooler or water pump. Q: What’s the difference between the WIX 24070 and the CAT 435-5142? A: The WIX 24070 is slightly taller at 5.25 inches, while the CAT 435-5142 is more compact at 4⅜ inches. The WIX filter is lighter (about 1 lb) and designed for smooth, high-flow operation, while the CAT filter is heavier (about 2 lb) with a thicker steel housing for extreme durability. Q: Which filter do most customers use? A: The WIX 24070 is our standard recommendation for most Sinister Diesel coolant filtration kits. It’s affordable, widely available, and requires no additives—just pure debris filtration and easy maintenance. Q: When should I consider using the CAT 435-5142 instead? A: The CAT 435-5142 is a great upgrade for heavy-duty or high-vibration applications. If your truck tows frequently, runs in harsh conditions, or you prefer OEM-grade construction, the CAT filter’s heavier shell and reinforced core are a solid choice. Q: Does either filter add coolant additives or chemicals? A: No—both filters are non-pelleted. That means they don’t release Supplemental Coolant Additives (SCAs). If you’re running Extended Life Coolant (ELC), that’s perfect—no extra maintenance needed. Q: Are both filters compatible with Sinister Diesel kits? A: Absolutely. Both the WIX 24070 and CAT 435-5142 thread directly into Sinister Diesel coolant filtration heads (11/16-16 thread). Fitment is perfect on Powerstroke, Cummins, and Duramax applications. Q: Which one is better for a limited space under the hood? A: The CAT 435-5142 is shorter at 4⅜", so it’s ideal if your installation area has less vertical clearance. (Which is why it's the only choice for some kits) Q: Which filter provides better coolant flow? A: The WIX 24070 typically offers slightly higher flow due to its taller design and lighter internal construction, making it ideal for daily drivers. Q: How often should these filters be replaced? A: Replace the first filter after 500 miles to remove initial debris from the system. Then replace the second filter around 1,000 miles, and the third one at 3,000 miles. After that, change the coolant filter with every oil filter service to keep your cooling system performing at its best. These intervals apply to both the WIX 24070 and CAT 435-5142. Q: What’s the bottom line better filter? WIX 24070: Best all-around filter for most trucks — easy, reliable, and affordable. (Most sold filter for Sinister Diesel Kits) CAT 435-5142: Heavy-duty option for extreme use or customers who prefer CAT’s OEM-grade toughness. Both keep your cooling system clean, protected, and performing at its best.

title: Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration System (w/ CAT) for 2003-2007 Ford Powerstroke 6.0L
description: Features: CNC Machined 6061 Aluminum Filter Block High Strength, Abrasion Resistant Silicon Hoses Extends the life of your Water Pump, Oil Cooler, and EGR Cooler Maintains optimal coolant flow as this is a bypass filtration kit 1 Year Warranty Includes: Genuine CAT Coolant Filter with 99% efficiency @ 50 Microns Filter Block Mounting Bracket Silicone Hoses Installation Hardware It doesn't matter if your 6.0L Powerstroke engine is brand new or has had a long service life, there are contaminants like casting sands leftover from the manufacturing process, metal shavings, rust, and mineral deposits floating in your coolant that can diminish the heat capacity of your truck's cooling system. Over time, these dangerous deposits can even cause damage to your water pump bearings and t he small passages inside your EGR cooler and oil cooler. The Filter Block attaches to a supplied bracket, which mounts to the factory holes so no drilling is required. This kit includes all the fittings needed for installation. The filter can be easily unscrewed for replacement. We recommend replacement of the filter at 500 miles with replacement of the second and third filters at 3,000 miles each. After 3 changes, filter replacements can be done once a year. The silicone hose fittings are pre-assembled for simple, leak-proof connections that are factory tested at twice the recommended radiator cap pressure. This kit comes with the upgraded CAT Coolant Filter that's 99% efficient @ 50 Microns. · Q: What is the installation time for this product? · A: The Sinister Diesel Coolant Filter Bypass system takes approximately 2 hours for installation. Installation time may vary based on experience Q: What coolant filters work best with Sinister Diesel coolant filtration kits? A: The two most popular and proven options are the WIX 24070 and the CAT 435-5142. Both deliver excellent protection against casting sand, rust, and debris that can clog your heater core, EGR cooler, Oil Cooler or water pump. Q: What’s the difference between the WIX 24070 and the CAT 435-5142? A: The WIX 24070 is slightly taller at 5.25 inches, while the CAT 435-5142 is more compact at 4⅜ inches. The WIX filter is lighter (about 1 lb) and designed for smooth, high-flow operation, while the CAT filter is heavier (about 2 lb) with a thicker steel housing for extreme durability. Q: Which filter do most customers use? A: The WIX 24070 is our standard recommendation for most Sinister Diesel coolant filtration kits. It’s affordable, widely available, and requires no additives—just pure debris filtration and easy maintenance. Q: When should I consider using the CAT 435-5142 instead? A: The CAT 435-5142 is a great upgrade for heavy-duty or high-vibration applications. If your truck tows frequently, runs in harsh conditions, or you prefer OEM-grade construction, the CAT filter’s heavier shell and reinforced core are a solid choice. Q: Does either filter add coolant additives or chemicals? A: No—both filters are non-pelleted. That means they don’t release Supplemental Coolant Additives (SCAs). If you’re running Extended Life Coolant (ELC), that’s perfect—no extra maintenance needed. Q: Are both filters compatible with Sinister Diesel kits? A: Absolutely. Both the WIX 24070 and CAT 435-5142 thread directly into Sinister Diesel coolant filtration heads (11/16-16 thread). Fitment is perfect on Powerstroke, Cummins, and Duramax applications. Q: Which one is better for a limited space under the hood? A: The CAT 435-5142 is shorter at 4⅜", so it’s ideal if your installation area has less vertical clearance. (Which is why it's the only choice for some kits) Q: Which filter provides better coolant flow? A: The WIX 24070 typically offers slightly higher flow due to its taller design and lighter internal construction, making it ideal for daily drivers. Q: How often should these filters be replaced? A: Replace the first filter after 500 miles to remove initial debris from the system. Then replace the second filter around 1,000 miles, and the third one at 3,000 miles. After that, change the coolant filter with every oil filter service to keep your cooling system performing at its best. These intervals apply to both the WIX 24070 and CAT 435-5142. Q: What’s the bottom line better filter? WIX 24070: Best all-around filter for most trucks — easy, reliable, and affordable. (Most sold filter for Sinister Diesel Kits) CAT 435-5142: Heavy-duty option for extreme use or customers who prefer CAT’s OEM-grade toughness. Both keep your cooling system clean, protected, and performing at its best.

title: ARP Head Stud Kit for Ford Powerstroke 2011-2016 6.7L
description: For all ARP Stud Purchases, Billing and Shipping addresses must match. ***All Sales are final for ARP Head Stud Kits, No Returns Signature Required upon Delivery It is for good reason that virtually every top professional engine builder relies on ARP Pro Series head studs for their all-out competition powerplants. Simply stated, there's not a better stud setup on the market today. For openers, ARP only uses premium grade 8740 alloy and a proprietart ARP2000 alloy that are rated far superior to "aircraft" quality alloy steels. Secondly, each stud is placed vertically into special racks and precisely heat-treated to 200,000 psi for the 8740 material and 220,000 psi for the ARP2000 material. This procedure ensures complete heat penetration and the results are far superior to those lesser quality studs from other manufacturers who just dump pieces into a basket and hope for the best. Following heat-treat, each stud is centerless ground to make sure it is as close to perfectly concentric as possible. This procedure involves about ten very slight cuts and results in an exceptionally straight part. It's important to note lesser quality studs are not even centerless ground - the material is thread rolled in bar stock form (mostly before heat-treat, when the material is easier to machine). Because ARP studs are manufactured to such exacting tolerances, you will note that gaskets and cylinder heads literally glide into position and are perfectly aligned - something that won't happen with inferior quality head studs. ARP studs are thread rolled after heat-treat, which gives them about 2000% (that's twenty times) better fatigue strength than those studs that are threaded prior to heat-treat (a very common industry practice). It costs a lot more to do it this way, because it's tough on tooling, but the results are well worth the extra effort. Premium quality 8740 and ARP2000 head stud kits are available for most all engines. You won't find a better quality stud on the market from any other source. Look for ARP stamped on each stud as your assurance of quality. Clearly, they are the best on the market today, and the favorite of leading professional engine builders in all forms of racing.

title: Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration System for 2001-2010 GM Duramax 6.6L LB7/LLY/LBZ/LMM
description: Add years of life to your diesel engine with this Coolant Filter Bypass System. Removing contaminates such as the casting sand and metal shavings means your engine's water pump, EGR cooler and oil cooler will last longer. title: Sinister Diesel Cold Side Charge Pipe for 2011-2016 Ford Powerstroke 6.7L
description: 50-State Legal with CARB EO # D-721-12 Fitment Fits 2011–2016 Ford F-250 / F-350 / F-450 / F-550 6.7L Powerstroke Note: Compatible with Pickup and Cab & Chassis models. Not compatible with 2017+ model years. Key Features 3" Mandrel-Bent Aluminum Tubing – Replaces factory plastic piping with rigid, high-strength aluminum. Up to 27% Less Restriction vs. Stock – Verified through independent airflow testing. No-Slip Lip Silicone Boot – Internal ridge locks onto the pipe bead to help prevent boost blow-off. 5-Ply High-Performance Silicone Boot – Designed for durability under elevated boost pressure. 3/4" Wide Stainless Steel T-Bolt Clamps – Heavy-duty clamping force for secure sealing. New O-Rings & Retaining Clips Included – No need to reuse fatigued factory components. TIG-Welded Aluminum Construction – Built for long-term durability and structural integrity. 2-Year Warranty – Backed by Sinister Diesel quality and support. Product Overview The factory cold-side charge pipe on the 2011–2016 6.7L Powerstroke is plastic. Over time, heat cycles, vibration, and boost pressure cause the plastic pipe to weaken, expand, or crack — especially under towing or tuned conditions. Plastic expands under boost. Aluminum does not. Sinister Diesel replaces the failure-prone factory cold-side pipe with a full 3" mandrel-bent, TIG-welded aluminum charge pipe engineered for strength and airflow efficiency. Independent airflow testing confirms up to 27% less restriction compared to factory piping across the usable airflow range. Lower restriction improves charge system efficiency and helps maintain consistent boost delivery under load. Why 3" Instead of 3.5"? Some aftermarket options advertise oversized 3.5" tubing. While larger diameter may sound appealing, airflow performance is not determined by size alone. Charge air systems must balance diameter, air velocity, and turbo response. Oversizing tubing beyond what the turbo and intercooler can efficiently support can reduce air velocity and increase system volume without delivering measurable gains. Sinister Diesel engineered this cold-side pipe at 3" to optimize airflow efficiency, maintain proper air velocity, and support real-world towing and tuned applications — without sacrificing boost response. This diameter provides substantial airflow improvement over the restrictive factory plastic pipe while preserving system balance and drivability. More aluminum means more strength. Some aftermarket kits rely heavily on silicone sections instead of full aluminum construction. Silicone can expand and billow under boost, affecting airflow consistency. Aluminum maintains its shape under pressure, preserving clean airflow and improving long-term durability. To further prevent boost leaks, our silicone boot features a molded No-Slip Lip — an internal ridge that mechanically locks onto the pipe bead. description: CARB EO # D-721-8 Currently ONLY valid for 2017-2019 Ford F250 F350 6.7L Powerstroke 50 State Legal. Compatible with 2017–2022 F-450 and F-550 Cab & Chassis models. Not compatible with 2023+ F-450 or F-550 Cab & Chassis models. Key Features 3" Mandrel-Bent Aluminum Tubing – Replaces factory plastic and rubber piping with rigid, high-strength aluminum. Up to 27% Less Restriction vs. Stock – Verified through independent airflow testing. No-Slip Lip Silicone Boot – Internal ridge locks onto the pipe bead to help prevent boost blow-off. 5-Ply High-Performance Silicone Boot – Designed for durability under elevated boost pressure. 3/4" Wide Stainless Steel T-Bolt Clamps – Heavy-duty clamping force for secure sealing. New O-Rings & Retaining Clips Included – No need to reuse fatigued factory components. TIG-Welded Aluminum Construction – Built for long-term durability and structural integrity. 2-Year Warranty – Backed by Sinister Diesel quality and support. Product Overview The factory cold-side charge pipe on the 2017–2026 6.7L Powerstroke is constructed from a combination of plastic and rubber sections. While designed for cost efficiency and flexibility, these materials expand under boost pressure and repeated heat cycles. It is common for the factory plastic connector near the throttle valve to crack, or for the rubber section to split over time — creating a massive boost leak and sudden loss of power. Rubber expands. Plastic fatigues. Aluminum maintains its shape. Under towing, heavy load, or tuned conditions, expansion in the factory plastic and rubber piping can disrupt airflow consistency and increase the likelihood of boost leaks. Sinister Diesel replaces the factory assembly with a full 3" mandrel-bent, TIG-welded aluminum charge pipe engineered for structural strength and airflow efficiency. Independent airflow testing confirms up to 27% less restriction compared to factory piping across the usable airflow range. Lower restriction improves overall charge system efficiency and helps maintain consistent boost delivery under load. Why 3" Instead of 3.5"? Some aftermarket options advertise oversized 3.5" tubing. While larger diameter may sound appealing, airflow performance is not determined by size alone. Charge air systems must balance diameter, air velocity, and turbo response. Oversizing tubing beyond what the turbo and intercooler can efficiently support can reduce air velocity and increase system volume without delivering measurable gains. Sinister Diesel engineered this cold-side pipe at 3" to optimize airflow efficiency, maintain proper air velocity, and support real-world towing and tuned applications — without sacrificing boost response. This diameter provides substantial airflow improvement over the restrictive factory plastic and rubber assembly while preserving system balance and drivability. To further prevent boost leaks, our silicone boot features a molded No-Slip Lip — an internal ridge that mechanically locks onto the pipe bead. Combined with our 3/4" wide stainless steel T-bolt clamps, this design helps prevent boot slippage under heavy boost or tuned applications. Unlike many competitors, Sinister Diesel includes new O-rings and retaining clips. Many upgrades require reusing factory sealing components that have been heat-cycled and fatigued. This is not a cosmetic upgrade. It is a structural and airflow improvement designed to eliminate weak points in the charge system. A: Unlike the factory full-flow filter, a bypass filter diverts a small portion of oil through a high-efficiency secondary filter, removing much finer particles (down to 2 microns). title: FASS Titanium Signature Series Diesel Fuel Lift Pump for 2017-2021 Ford Powerstroke 6.7L (140gph)
description: All FASS Product Sales are Final - Non Refundable The Titanium Signature Series diesel fuel system was engineered by FASS Diesel Fuel Systems to be the top performing, longest lasting and most reliable aftermarket filtration system in the diesel industry. Rated at 140 gallons per hour and backed by our limited lifetime warranty the Titanium Signature Series fuel system can support your Ford Powerstroke 6.7L diesel engine with enough fuel for up to 700 HP. A Titanium Signature Series fuel system also offers advanced filtration benefits that will help protect your Powerstroke’s injection systems, effectively extending the life of both your Injection Pump and Injectors. These Benefits include an improved filtration rating of 3 Microns, a built-in water separation filter and FASS Diesel Fuel Systems patented air/vapor removal process. Powerstroke diesel engines are known for having injector failure issues; this can be caused by several different factors and FASS Filtration Systems is designed to eliminate those that are fuel related. The Titanium Signature Series diesel fuel systems are the superior fuel pump and filtration system, in the diesel industry. This is because here at FASS Diesel Fuel Systems we focus on perfecting our pumps and how they are engineered, taking the extra steps that our competitors ignore while they rush to get product out the door. By doing this we have less than a 2% failure rating with our motor and a design that implements unique design features you can only get with FASS Diesel Fuel Systems FASS Whisper Technology (FWT) was engineered to meet the stringent demands of today’s quiet diesel engines. FASS Whisper Technology was in development and testing for over two and a half years to meet FASS Fuel System’s high quality and durability standards. FASS Whisper Technology effectively and drastically reduces the sound levels of the Signature Series FASS Diesel Fuel System to a “whisper”while at the same time increasing fuel flow rates. Our competition reduces noise levels by reducing flow rates. Performance Radius Cuts (PRC) are an advanced machining technique used to improve the flow of fuel through the FASS System by creating smooth flowing cuts within the aluminum housing. This prevents the reintroduction of air and vapor back into the fuel and to prevent eddy currents from forming within the corner of a 90-degree sharp angle. FASS Titanium Signature Fuel Systems are the only Fuel Air Separation System that incorporates these exclusive Performance Radius Cuts, lesser competitors commonly use T-Bone and square 90-degree fittings that can limit fuel flow, cause restrictions and reintroduce air back into your fuel. Mass Flow Return (MFR) technology is used to continually polish your fuel by keeping a positive pressure on the fuel being delivered to the engine to prevent cavitation along with galling and scoring. Then returning the fuel, that’s not yet needed, back to the tank after it has been passed through the filtration process, removing dirt, air, vapor and water. This means your fuel is being constantly “Polished” (cleaned), guaranteeing that your diesel truck always has the cleanest fuel possible. The MFR in combination with the optional FASS Fuel Heater options will help insure “EXTREME” cold weather operations! Bolt On Bracket Mounts old mounting styles required drilling or welding when mounting your new pump. title: FASS Titanium Signature Series Diesel Fuel Lift Pump for 2011-2016 Ford Powerstroke 6.7L (140gph)
description: All FASS Product Sales are Final - Non Refundable FASS Whisper Technology (FWT) was engineered to meet stringent demands of today's quiet diesel engines. FASS Whisper Technology was in development and tested for over two and a half years to meet FASS Fuel System's high quality and durability standards. FASS Whisper Technology effectively and drastically reduces the sound levels of the Signature Series FASS Diesel Fuel System to a "whisper" while at the same time increases fuel flow rates. Performance Radius Cuts (PRC) are an advanced machining technique used to improve the flow of fuel through the FASS System by creating smooth flowing cuts within the aluminum housing. This prevents the reintroduction of air and vapor back into the fuel and to prevent eddy currents from forming within the corner of a 90-degree sharp angle. FASS Titanium Signature Fuel Systems are the only Fuel Air Separation System that incorporates these exclusive Performance Radius Cuts, lesser competitors commonly use T-Bone and square 90-degree fittings that can limit fuel flow, causing restriction and reintroducing air back into your fuel. Mass Flow Return (MFR) technology is used to continually polish your fuel by keeping a positive pressure on the fuel being delivered to the engine to prevent cavitation along with galling and scoring. Then returning the fuel, that's not yet needed, back to the tank after it has been passed through the filtration process, removing dirt, air vapor and water. This means your fuel is being constantly "Polished" (cleaned), guaranteeing that your diesel truck always has the cleanest fuel possible. The MFR in combination with the optional FASS Fuel Heater options will help insure extreme cold weather operations! Bolt On Bracket Mounts old mounting styles required drilling or welding then mounting your new pump. This has now been simplified with our new bolt on brackets that secure your truck bed to your frame. Mounting your Titanium Signature Series system takes only minutes and all the brackets are made from machined steel for superior support and strength. FASS Titanium Signature Fuel Systems are the only Fuel Air Separation System that incorporates these exclusive Performance Radius Cuts, lesser competitors commonly use T-Bone and square 90-degree fittings that can limit fuel flow, causing restriction and reintroducing air back into your fuel. Mass Flow Return (MFR) technology is used to continually polish your fuel by keeping a positive pressure on the fuel being delivered to the engine to prevent cavitation along with galling and scoring. Then returning the fuel, that's not yet needed, back to the tank after it has been passed through the filtration process, removing dirt, air vapor and water. This means your fuel is being constantly "Polished" (cleaned), guaranteeing that your diesel truck always has the cleanest fuel possible. The MFR in combination with the optional FASS Fuel Heater options will help insure extreme cold weather operations! Bolt On Bracket Mounts old mounting styles required drilling or welding then mounting your new pump. This has now been simplified with our new bolt on brackets that secure your truck bed to your frame. Mounting your Titanium Signature Series system takes only minutes and all the brackets are made from machined steel for superior support and strength. It uses your stock mounting points, oil lines, and exhaust connections—making installation quick, simple, and hassle-free while providing a major upgrade in airflow and reliability. A: Every Pitbull Series® Turbo features our Signature Sinister Blue powder coat for corrosion resistance, heat protection, and a clean, custom look. Q: Is this turbo a direct bolt-on? A: Yes. Never worry about that again with a Sinister Diesel Fuel Filter Cap. Constructed from a single solid aluminum billet, the Sinister Diesel Fuel Filter Cap is far stronger than the OEM plastic cap. It's anodized in Sinister Blue for corrosion resistance and features a 1/8th" NPT port for testing fuel pressure. Finished in Sinister Blue, this intake doesn’t just perform, it looks great under the hood. Why Buy From Sinister Diesel? Since 2009, Sinister Diesel has been the trusted source for Cummins, Powerstroke, and Duramax upgrades. Every intake is engineered in-house, tested in real-world conditions, and backed by our 2 Year Warranty. When you buy Sinister, you’re getting proven performance, premium materials, and unmatched reliability. What’s Included (1) CNC Mandrel-Bent Aluminum Intake Tube (1) Reusable Cotton Media Filter Installation Hardware Click Here for Pre-Filter FAQs: Q: I know it is stronger and looks better, but doesn't aluminum conduct more heat than plastic? A: On a technical standpoint, aluminum would be a little more subject to the heat because it is metal, but, it is thin walled tubing, and would also dissipate the heat much faster as air is moving. The issue with a plastic intake, while it takes just as much time to heat up, it takes a considerable amount more time to dissipate that heat build up, also known as heat soak. We could easily out source our intakes like most other companies, but instead we choose to spend more time and effort to make these out of a much better material, in house with our own welders and materials. We have been testing and running these for quite some time, and have out performed every other intake we have put it up against.

title: Mishimoto Air-to-Water Performance Intercooler for 2011-2016 Ford Powerstroke 6.7L - Black
description: The 2011–2016 Ford Super Duty equipped with the 6.7L Powerstroke is one of the most capable and reliable trucks on the road today. But, when Mishimoto heard about 6.7L Powerstroke air-to-water intercoolers leaking internally, we set to work making the 6.7L Powerstroke even better. The Mishimoto 6.7L Powerstroke intercooler brings even more reliability to the 6.7L by ditching the stamped and stacked plate stock design. Instead, our intercooler is built with cast aluminum end tanks that are precision TIG-welded to the robust bar-and-plate core. The cast end-tanks reduce potential failure points, making them more reliable than cut and welded tanks. Aside from being more durable than stock, the Mishimoto 6.7L Powerstroke intercooler also flows better. Flow bench testing showed that the Mishimoto intercooler flowed 22% better than the stock cooler . On the dyno, that extra flow allowed our stock 6.7L Powerstroke to make 40 lb-ft more torque . Like all Mishimoto products, the 2011–2016 Ford 6.7L Powerstroke Air-to-Water intercooler is covered by the Mishimoto Lifetime Warranty. Features: Direct fit for all 2011–2016 Ford 6.7L Powerstrokes 22% greater airflow than stock 6.7L Powerstroke intercooler Dyno-proven 40 lb-ft torque gains without other modifications Cast end tanks are more durable than brazed or welded end tanks, eliminating potential leak points Bar-and-plate core for efficient cooling and unsurpassed strength No tune required–intercooler can be installed on a completely stock or modified truck Finished in Stealthy Micro-Wrinkle Black Patent pending Mishimoto Lifetime Warranty

description: Sinister Diesel Bypass Oil Filter System – 6.7L Cummins Fits 2007.5–2012 Dodge Ram 2500 / 3500 with 6.7L Cummins Diesel Key Features • Uses Amsoil EaBP100 Absolute Efficiency 2-Micron Bypass Filter – captures particles down to 2 microns for superior protection • CNC-machined billet aluminum oil cap and filter block for precision fit and long-term durability • Braided stainless steel lines with AN fittings for leak-free reliability • Removes soot, metal shavings, and fine contaminants your stock filter can’t • Helps extend injector, bearing, and turbocharger life • Improves overall oil cleanliness and lubrication efficiency • Complete kit with pre-made lines, bracket, and hardware • Backed by a 2-Year Sinister Diesel Warranty Product Overview Protect your 6.7L Cummins with the Sinister Diesel Bypass Oil Filter System—designed to deliver cleaner oil, reduced wear, and longer engine life. The factory filter only captures larger particles, allowing fine soot and contaminants to stay in circulation. This system solves that by adding an Amsoil EaBP100 2-micron bypass filter capable of removing the ultra-fine debris your OEM filter can’t catch. Built using CNC-machined billet aluminum components and high-quality stainless braided lines, the kit ensures durability and leak-free operation even in demanding conditions. By improving oil cleanliness, you help extend the service life of injectors, bearings, turbochargers, and other high-precision components. Ideal for towing, daily driving, or performance use, this system provides long-lasting engine protection and improved maintenance efficiency. What’s Included • (1) Amsoil EaBP100 2-Micron Bypass Filter • (1) Sinister Diesel Billet Aluminum Oil Cap • (1) Sinister Diesel Billet Filter Block • Braided Stainless Lines with AN Fittings • Mounting Bracket & Installation Hardware Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) Q: How often should I replace the bypass oil filter? A: Replace the Amsoil EaBP100 every 15,000 miles or once per year, whichever comes first. Q: Does this replace the factory oil filter? A: No. This system works alongside the OEM filter to provide finer, more efficient filtration. Q: Will I need to modify my truck? A: No cutting or drilling is required. This is a complete bolt-on system. Q: What benefits will I notice? A: Cleaner oil, reduced engine wear, extended oil life, and longer-lasting injectors, bearings, and turbo components. Q: What efficiency does the bypass filter provide? A: The EaBP100 filters down to 2 microns at 98.7% efficiency—far better than stock filters, which typically stop around 20 microns. Q: Is this kit compatible with dual alternator trucks? A: No, this system does not fit dual alternator configurations. Click Here for the Install Guide

title: FASS Titanium Signature Series Diesel Fuel Lift Pump for 2019-2020 Dodge Cummins 6.7L (100gph)
description: All FASS Product Sales are Final - Non Refundable FASS Whisper Technology (FWT) was engineered to meet stringent demands of today's quiet diesel engines. FASS Whisper Technology was in development and tested for over two and a half years to meet FASS Fuel System's high quality and durability standards. FASS Whisper Technology effectively and drastically reduces the sound levels of the Signature Series FASS Diesel Fuel System to a "whisper" while at the same time increases fuel flow rates. Performance Radius Cuts (PRC) are an advanced machining technique used to improve the flow of fuel through the FASS System by creating smooth flowing cuts within the aluminum housing. This prevents the reintroduction of air and vapor back into the fuel and to prevent eddy currents from forming within the corner of a 90-degree sharp angle. FASS Titanium Signature Fuel Systems are the only Fuel Air Separation System that incorporates these exclusive Performance Radius Cuts, lesser competitors commonly use T-Bone and square 90-degree fittings that can limit fuel flow, causing restriction and reintroducing air back into your fuel. Mass Flow Return (MFR) technology is used to continually polish your fuel by keeping a positive pressure on the fuel being delivered to the engine to prevent cavitation along with galling and scoring. Then returning the fuel, that's not yet needed, back to the tank after it has been passed through the filtration process, removing dirt, air vapor and water. This means your fuel is being constantly "Polished" (cleaned), guaranteeing that your diesel truck always has the cleanest fuel possible. The MFR in combination with the optional FASS Fuel Heater options will help insure extreme cold weather operations! Bolt On Bracket Mounts old mounting styles required drilling or welding then mounting your new pump. This has now been simplified with our new bolt on brackets that secure your truck bed to your frame. Mounting your Titanium Signature Series system takes only minutes and all the brackets are made from machined steel for superior support and strength. Features: New Bolt On Bracket Installation Includes Hardware/Install Kit Complete Replacement Pump 100 Gallons Per Hour Flow Rating Removes Water, Air and Debris from Diesel Fuel 2 Micron Filtration Rating Protects and Extends the life of your injectors Improved Cold Weather Starts Decreases Carbon Monoxide and Carbon Dioxide Emissions 1/2 Inch Fuel Lines and Ports Fuel Mileage Improvement Improved Fuel Volume Delivery Smoother Engine Idle Optional Electric Heater Ports Optional Fuel Pressure Gauge Port Limited Lifetime Warranty 30,000 Mile Filter Life Expectancy Quietest Motor On The Market

title: Sinister Diesel Blue Spring Kit with Adj. Billet Spring Housing and Fuel Filter Cap for 2003-2007
description: .two-column { display:flex; flex-direction:row; } @media only screen and (max-width: 600px) { .two-column, .two-column.reverse { flex-direction:column; } } .two-column > div { flex:1; margin:10px; max-width: 700px } CARB EO# D-721-4 The easy 30 minute install of the new updated "Blue Spring" will increase your fuel pressure back up to where it should be from the factory. Most 6.0's see an increase of 10 psi of fuel pressure. The added fuel pressure will help keep the injectors from outflowing the stock fuel pump, meaning no air in the injectors, and an injector that will live much longer. Most trucks will actually quite down a little at idle, rev up a lot quicker and with the added fuel pressure, a little quicker spool up on the turbo. The kit does include all the O-Rings and hard parts to rebuild the fuel pressure housing. Another major bonus is that this block has an adjuster screw in it. This will allow you to bump pressure up a little for big injectors. Or after the spring settles after years of abuse, just loosen up the jam nut, and run the adjuster screw in a turn or two to get your fuel pressure back to where you want it. Everybody has their own opinion of fuel pressure in a 6.0L Powerstroke, why not choose for yourself, we recommend setting it between 55-68psi. This kit also comes with a Brand New Billet Aluminum housing to keep the regulator spring set and to prevent leaks from the housing. This is a much thicker, stronger billet piece than the factory Cast Aluminum spring housing. The factory unit is known to leak or "weep" fuel from the sides. This kit will also help clean up the look of the factory filter housing as well. A cracked fuel filter cap can lead to air in the fuel system. That can result in hard starting or potential fuel injector issues. A cracked fuel filter cap can also cause a fuel leak, which is definitely a bad idea on a hot engine. Never worry about that again with a Sinister Diesel Fuel Filter Cap. Constructed from a single solid aluminum billet, the Sinister Diesel Fuel Filter Cap is far stronger than the OEM plastic cap. It's anodized in Sinister Blue for corrosion resistance and features a 1/8th" NPT port for testing fuel pressure. Features: Anodized in Sinister Blue CNC Machined from T6-6061 billet aluminum Laser etched Sinister Diesel logo New "Blue Spring" New Gaskets Impervious to cracking like the fuel filter cap Adjustable fuel pressure so you can fine-tune your performance Fuel Filter Cap features a 1/8th" NPT port for fuel pressure testing Restores lost fuel pressure from a worn spring Helps save Injectors from low fuel pressure 2 Year Warranty Includes: (1) Sinister Diesel Billet Spring Housing (1) Sinister Diesel Fuel Filter Cap (1) New "Blue Spring" (1) New Gasket/O-ring set Installation Hardware Note: NOT Compatible with E-series or Cab and Chassis
