title: Sinister Diesel Blue EGR Cooler for 2004-2007 Ford Powerstroke 6.0L
description: Sinister Diesel EGR Cooler | 2004–2007 Ford Powerstroke 6.0L Key Features • 50-state legal replacement EGR cooler with CARB EO# D-721 • Stainless steel internal tube design eliminates factory fin-style failure points • Fully TIG-welded and pressure tested for long-term reliability • Improved exhaust gas flow compared to the factory cooler • Direct-fit replacement designed specifically for the 6.0L Powerstroke • Compatible with stock ECM and all performance tuners • Proven solution trusted by tens of thousands of 6.0L Powerstroke owners • Backed by a 2-year warranty Product Overview Sinister Diesel has completely re-engineered the EGR cooler for the 6.0L Powerstroke to address the most common and costly failure points found in the factory design. The OEM EGR cooler relies on narrow internal cooling fins joined by brazed connections. When those brazed joints crack, coolant can be drawn into the intake manifold and combustion chamber, often leading to blown head gaskets, hydro-lock, and extremely expensive engine repairs. The Sinister Diesel EGR Cooler replaces the restrictive fin-style core with high-flow stainless steel tubing that is fully TIG welded for superior strength and durability. This design dramatically reduces the risk of internal failure, clogging, and coolant intrusion while improving exhaust gas flow and long-term reliability. This isn’t a theoretical upgrade — tens of thousands of satisfied 6.0L Powerstroke owners are running Sinister Diesel EGR coolers every day in work trucks, tow rigs, and daily drivers. The design has been proven over years of real-world use to deliver reliability the factory cooler simply can’t match. Designed to match factory fitment, this EGR cooler installs as a direct replacement on 2004–2007 Ford F-250, F-350 Super Duty, and Ford Excursion models equipped with the 6.0L Powerstroke. It works seamlessly with the factory ECM and remains fully compatible with all performance tuners on the market. Each cooler is extensively tested at Sinister Diesel’s California headquarters and is certified 50-state legal under CARB EO# D-721, giving you peace of mind whether you’re returning a truck to emissions compliance, preparing it for resale, or simply replacing a failed factory unit with a more reliable solution. Important Fitment Note Some early 2004 6.0L Powerstroke engines were equipped with a round-style EGR cooler instead of the later square-style design. This EGR cooler is intended for square-style applications. Please verify which EGR cooler style your vehicle is equipped with before ordering to ensure proper fitment. What’s Included • Sinister Diesel EGR Cooler for 2004–2007 Ford Powerstroke 6.0L Frequently Asked Questions Q: Is this EGR cooler 50-state legal? A: Yes. This EGR cooler is CARB certified under EO# D-721 and is legal for use in all 50 states. Q: Do I need to remove the intake manifold to install this EGR cooler? A: Yes. The intake manifold does need to be removed to properly install the Sinister Diesel EGR Cooler. To make installation easier and ensure a proper seal during reassembly, we strongly recommend replacing the intake gaskets at the same time. Sinister Diesel offers a replacement intake gasket kit specifically for this job. Q: Will this work with stock tuning or aftermarket tuners? A: Yes. This EGR cooler is fully compatible with the factory ECM and works with all performance tuners without requiring any modifications. Q: How is this different from the factory EGR cooler? A: The factory cooler uses fin-style passages with brazed joints that are prone to cracking and clogging. The Sinister Diesel EGR Cooler uses stainless steel tubing with TIG welds, greatly improving durability, flow, and long-term reliability. Q: How do I know if my truck has a round or square EGR cooler? A: Some early 2004 models used a round-style EGR cooler, while later 2004–2007 trucks use a square-style cooler. A visual inspection of the EGR cooler or confirmation by VIN/build date is recommended before ordering. If you’re unsure, our support team can help verify fitment. Q: What vehicles does this fit? A: This EGR cooler fits 2004, 2005, 2006, and 2007 Ford F-250, F-350 Super Duty, and Ford Excursion models equipped with the 6.0L Powerstroke diesel engine that use the square-style EGR cooler.

title: Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration System for 2019-2024 Ram 2500/3500 Cummins 6.7L
description: Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration System – 2019–2024 Ram 2500/3500 6.7 Cummins Diesel Why You Need This Kit: Protects your heater core — the most common coolant failure point on Cummins trucks Filters out casting sand, metal shavings, and debris that the factory system can’t catch Keeps coolant passages clean, extending the life of your water pump, oil cooler, and EGR cooler Prevents costly breakdowns by stopping contamination before it reaches critical components Easy upkeep — filters are quick to replace with simple, tool-free service FREE SHIPPING Description: The Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration Kit is the best insurance you can buy for your 6.7L Cummins. The factory cooling system allows casting sand, rust, and metal debris to circulate, which clogs the heater core and shortens the life of vital parts like the water pump, oil cooler, and EGR cooler. Our kit solves this by continuously filtering small amounts of coolant while the engine runs, trapping harmful contaminants before they do damage. Engineered from CNC-machined billet 6061 aluminum and finished in signature Sinister Blue, this system is built to last. It mounts directly to factory holes (no drilling required) and comes with reinforced silicone hoses that are pre-assembled for leak-proof connections tested to twice the normal system pressure. Maintenance is simple: replace the first filter at 500 miles, the second at 1,500 miles, and then every oil change interval thereafter. What’s Included: Genuine WIX Coolant Filter Sinister Diesel Billet Filter Block Mounting Bracket Reinforced Silicone Hoses (pre-assembled) Installation Hardware Features: CNC-Machined 6061 Aluminum, anodized in Sinister Blue Cleaner coolant = longer engine life Leak-proof, reinforced silicone hoses 1-Year Warranty Coolant Filter Q&A for Sinister Diesel 2019–2024 6.7L Cummins Coolant Filtration Kit What filter does this kit use? The Sinister Diesel 6.7 Cummins Coolant Filtration Kit includes a WIX 24070 spin-on coolant filter. It traps casting sand, rust, and metal shavings that cause heater core, EGR cooler, and oil cooler failures. Why a bypass filter instead of inline? The system filters a small percentage of coolant each time the engine runs until all coolant has been cleaned. An inline filter could block flow if it ever clogged, but our bypass design has a built-in fail-safe—coolant still circulates normally with zero risk to the engine or components. Why WIX? It offers the perfect balance of filtration and flow for the Cummins 6.7L system. The compact size fits our bracket perfectly, and the 27-micron media removes fine debris without affecting coolant pressure. Does it change coolant chemistry? No. The WIX 24070 is a non-chemical filter—no pellets or additives. It won’t alter your coolant blend or require SCA top-offs. How often should I replace the filter? • First change: 500 miles • Second: around 1,000 miles • Third: at 3,000 miles • After that: every oil filter service This schedule keeps your cooling system clean and prevents buildup in small coolant passages. Will it restrict coolant flow? No. The filter is engineered for high flow and low restriction, so your coolant pressure stays stable while debris is trapped safely inside the element. How does this protect my Cummins engine? By constantly cleaning the coolant, the system prevents abrasive debris from circulating and wearing out your water pump, heater core, or coolers—saving you from costly repairs. Quick Takeaway • Kit is designed specifically for Ram 2500/3500 Cummins • 27-micron filtration for debris control • Safe bypass design—no coolant blockage risk • Maintenance made easy with common WIX replacements • Extends cooling-system life and reliability

title: Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration System for 2003-2007 Ford Powerstroke 6.0L
description: .two-column { display:flex; flex-direction:row; } @media only screen and (max-width: 600px) { .two-column, .two-column.reverse { flex-direction:column; } } .two-column > div { flex:1; margin:10px; max-width: 700px } Features and Benefits: The new and improved Filter Block is CNC Machined from Solid Billet 6061 Aluminum High Strength, Abrasion Resistant Silicone Hoses with molded T for reduced point of failure Extends the life of your Water Pump, Oil Cooler, and EGR Cooler Maintains optimal coolant flow as this is a bypass filtration kit 1 Year Warranty It doesn't matter if your 6.0L Ford Powerstroke engine is brand new or has had a long service life, there are contaminants like casting sand leftover from the manufacturing process, metal shavings, rust, and mineral deposits floating in your coolant that can diminish the heat capacity of your truck's cooling system. Over time, these dangerous deposits can even cause damage to your water pump bearings and the small passages inside your EGR cooler and oil cooler. The CNC machined billet 6061 aluminum anodized construction filter boss attaches to a supplied bracket, which mounts to factory holes so no drilling is required. This kit includes all fittings needed for installation. The filter can be easily unscrewed for replacement. We recommend replacement of the first filter at 500 miles with replacement of the second and third filters is at 3,000 miles each. After 3 changes, filter replacement can be done once a year. The silicone hoses fittings are pre-assembled for simple, leak-proof connections that factory tested at twice the recommended radiator cap pressure. Includes: Genuine WIX Coolant Filter Filter Block Mounting Bracket Nickel plated ball valves Silicone Hoses Molded Silicone T-Hose Installation Hardware Q: What coolant filters work best with Sinister Diesel coolant filtration kits? A: The two most popular and proven options are the WIX 24070 and the CAT 435-5142. Both deliver excellent protection against casting sand, rust, and debris that can clog your heater core, EGR cooler, Oil Cooler or water pump. Q: What’s the difference between the WIX 24070 and the CAT 435-5142? A: The WIX 24070 is slightly taller at 5.25 inches, while the CAT 435-5142 is more compact at 4⅜ inches. The WIX filter is lighter (about 1 lb) and designed for smooth, high-flow operation, while the CAT filter is heavier (about 2 lb) with a thicker steel housing for extreme durability. Q: Which filter do most customers use? A: The WIX 24070 is our standard recommendation for most Sinister Diesel coolant filtration kits. It’s affordable, widely available, and requires no additives—just pure debris filtration and easy maintenance. Q: When should I consider using the CAT 435-5142 instead? A: The CAT 435-5142 is a great upgrade for heavy-duty or high-vibration applications. If your truck tows frequently, runs in harsh conditions, or you prefer OEM-grade construction, the CAT filter’s heavier shell and reinforced core are a solid choice. Q: Does either filter add coolant additives or chemicals? A: No—both filters are non-pelleted. That means they don’t release Supplemental Coolant Additives (SCAs). If you’re running Extended Life Coolant (ELC), that’s perfect—no extra maintenance needed. Q: Are both filters compatible with Sinister Diesel kits? A: Absolutely. Both the WIX 24070 and CAT 435-5142 thread directly into Sinister Diesel coolant filtration heads (11/16-16 thread). Fitment is perfect on Powerstroke, Cummins, and Duramax applications. Q: Which one is better for a limited space under the hood? A: The CAT 435-5142 is shorter at 4⅜", so it’s ideal if your installation area has less vertical clearance. (Which is why it's the only choice for some kits) Q: Which filter provides better coolant flow? A: The WIX 24070 typically offers slightly higher flow due to its taller design and lighter internal construction, making it ideal for daily drivers. Q: How often should these filters be replaced? A: Replace the first filter after 500 miles to remove initial debris from the system. Then replace the second filter around 1,000 miles, and the third one at 3,000 miles. After that, change the coolant filter with every oil filter service to keep your cooling system performing at its best. These intervals apply to both the WIX 24070 and CAT 435-5142. Q: What’s the bottom line better filter? A: WIX 24070: Best all-around filter for most trucks — easy, reliable, and affordable. (Most sold filter for Sinister Diesel Kits) CAT 435-5142: Heavy-duty option for extreme use or customers who prefer CAT’s OEM-grade toughness. Both keep your cooling system clean, protected, and performing at its best.

title: Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration System for 2013-2018 Dodge Cummins 6.7L
description: Features: Precision Engineering: Crafted with precision to fit the 2013-2018 Dodge Cummins 6.7L models, offering maximum performance and protection High-Quality Filter: Comes equipped with a high-quality filter designed to trap contaminants effectively Enhanced Protection: Protects your heater core from premature clogging and costly failure — catching harmful casting sand, metal shavings, and rust before they ever reach it. Durable Construction: Built with high-quality robust materials such as 6061 Billet Aluminum to withstand the tough environment of the engine bay, ensuring longevity and reliability Easy Installation: Easy bolt-on installation with durable, high-flow hoses and billet aluminum filter base built to last the life of your truck. Improved Coolant Flow: Extends heater core and EGR cooler life by removing abrasive debris that would otherwise embed and restrict coolant flow. 1 Year Warranty Benefits: Extended Engine Life: By filtering out contaminants, the system helps in extending the life of the engine and its components Reduced Maintenance Costs: Protects the cooling system from potential damage, reducing the likelihood of costly repairs Optimized Performance: Maintains optimal engine temperature, ensuring the vehicle operates at peak performance levels Peace of Mind: Offers assurance knowing that your engine's cooling system is well-protected against wear and damage Package Includes: WIX Coolant Filter Filter Block Mounting Bracket Silicone Hoses Installation Hardware Coolant Filter Q&A for Sinister Diesel 2013–2018 6.7L Cummins Coolant Filtration Kit What filter does this kit use? The Sinister Diesel 6.7 Cummins Coolant Filtration Kit includes a WIX 24070 spin-on coolant filter. It traps casting sand, rust, and metal shavings that cause heater core, EGR cooler, and oil cooler failures. Why a bypass filter instead of inline? The system filters a small percentage of coolant each time the engine runs until all coolant has been cleaned. An inline filter could block flow if it ever clogged, but our bypass design has a built-in fail-safe—coolant still circulates normally with zero risk to the engine or components. Why WIX? It offers the perfect balance of filtration and flow for the Cummins 6.7L system. The compact size fits our bracket perfectly, and the 27-micron media removes fine debris without affecting coolant pressure. Does it change coolant chemistry? No. The WIX 24070 is a non-chemical filter—no pellets or additives. It won’t alter your coolant blend or require SCA top-offs. How often should I replace the filter? • First change: 500 miles • Second: around 1,000 miles • Third: at 3,000 miles • After that: every oil filter service This schedule keeps your cooling system clean and prevents buildup in small coolant passages. Will it restrict coolant flow? No. The filter is engineered for high flow and low restriction, so your coolant pressure stays stable while debris is trapped safely inside the element. How does this protect my Cummins engine? By constantly cleaning the coolant, the system prevents abrasive debris from circulating and wearing out your water pump, heater core, or coolers—saving you from costly repairs. Key Features 3" Mandrel-Bent Aluminum Tubing – Larger than the factory 2.5" pipe to reduce restriction and increase airflow capacity. Up to 61% Less Restriction vs. Stock – Verified through independent airflow testing. TIG-Welded Aluminum Construction – Built for strength and long-term durability under boost. 3/4" Wide Stainless Steel T-Bolt Clamp – Heavy-duty clamping force for secure sealing under load. New O-Ring & Retaining Clip Included – Eliminates the need to reuse fatigued factory components. 2-Year Warranty – Backed by Sinister Diesel quality and support. Product Overview The factory hot-side charge pipe on the 6.7L Powerstroke measures only 2.5 inches in diameter, creating a restriction point as airflow demand increases. Under towing, heavy load, or tuned conditions, this bottleneck forces the turbo to work harder to move the same volume of air. Sinister Diesel upgrades the system to a full 3" mandrel-bent, TIG-welded aluminum pipe engineered to reduce restriction and improve charge system efficiency. Independent airflow testing confirms up to 61% less restriction compared to factory piping. Lower restriction means the turbo does not have to work as hard to reach target boost — improving overall system efficiency and allowing the truck to scale with increased airflow demand. This is not a cosmetic upgrade. It is a measurable airflow improvement. More aluminum means more strength. Some aftermarket options rely heavily on silicone sections instead of full aluminum construction. Silicone can expand under boost and affect airflow consistency. Aluminum maintains its shape under pressure, preserving consistent airflow characteristics and improving structural integrity. Unlike many competitors, Sinister Diesel includes a new O-ring and retaining clip. Many single-pipe upgrades require reusing factory sealing components that have been heat-cycled and fatigued, increasing the risk of boost leaks. No-Slip Lip Silicone Boots – Internal ridge locks the boot to the pipe to help prevent boost blow-off and slippage. 3/4" Wide Stainless Steel T-Bolt Clamps – Heavy-duty clamping force for secure sealing under high boost. Product Overview The factory charge pipe system on the 2011–2016 6.7L Powerstroke uses plastic and rubber components on the cold side and a restrictive 2.5" metal pipe on the hot side. After years of heat cycles, boost pressure, and vibration, these components are known to crack, separate, or develop boost leaks — especially on tuned or hard-working trucks. Sinister Diesel replaces both sides with full 3" mandrel-bent, TIG-welded aluminum tubing engineered for strength, airflow efficiency, and long-term durability. Independent airflow testing confirms measurable improvements over factory piping: Up to 61% less restriction on the hot side compared to stock Up to 27% less restriction on the cold side across the usable airflow range Lower restriction means the turbo does not have to work as hard to move the same volume of air. That improves charge system efficiency and supports increased airflow demand from towing, heavy load, or tuned applications. More aluminum tubing means a stronger, more reliable product. The factory cold-side pipe is plastic. Some aftermarket kits use minimal aluminum and heavily rely on silicone or rubber instead of full aluminum piping. Often only a short section is upgraded while long silicone sections are used because they are less expensive and easier to produce than mandrel-bent, TIG-welded, powder-coated aluminum tubing. Excessive silicone or rubber can expand and billow under heavy boost, disrupting clean airflow and reducing structural stability. Aluminum maintains its shape under pressure, preserving consistent airflow and minimizing expansion under load. Many competing kits require you to remove and reuse factory O-rings and retaining clips from your original charge pipes. These components are often heat-cycled and fatigued. Sinister Diesel includes new O-rings and retaining clips for both the hot and cold side connections — ensuring a proper seal and eliminating the risk of reinstalling worn sealing components. To further prevent boost leaks, our silicone boots feature a molded No-Slip Lip — an internal ridge that mechanically locks onto the pipe bead. Combined with our 3/4" wide stainless steel T-bolt clamps, this design helps prevent boot slippage under heavy boost or tuned conditions. This is not a cosmetic upgrade. It is a structural, airflow, and reliability improvement designed to eliminate bottlenecks and failure points in the charge system. A: The factory plastic charge pipe is prone to cracking and failure under heat and boost. This aluminum upgrade eliminates that weak point, providing long-term reliability and peace of mind. Will this fit my truck? A: This kit fits 2011–2016 Ford Super Duty trucks equipped with the 6.7L Powerstroke engine. It is designed for use with the factory turbocharger and intercooler. description: The Sinister Diesel Coolant Filter & Oil Filter Kit for 6.0L Ford Powerstroke is designed to extend the service life of your fluids by keeping both your oil and coolant free from tiny contaminants that can cause damage to your engine. Oil and Coolant are the life blood of your 6.0L Powerstroke engine and must be kept clean to ensure proper engine operation, lubrication, and even heat transfer. When these fluids are contaminated it can cause premature wear on engine and turbocharger bearings or even engine failure from a lack of lubrication or overheating due to a constriction within the fluid passages inside the block and engine components like your EGR Cooler. Whether your engine has been freshly rebuilt or has racked up hundreds of thousands of miles, contaminants like casting sand leftover from the manufacturing process, metal shavings, rust, mineral deposits floating in the coolant, or oil sludge are all but guaranteed and without treatment can spell disaster. With the Sinister Diesel Coolant Filter & Oil Filter Kit for the 2003-2007 6.0L Ford Powerstroke, you can be sure your both of these critical systems are operating at maximum efficiency. This Sinister Diesel Coolant Filter & Oil Filter Kit includes all mounts and fittings needed for installation and is machined from 6061 billet aluminum. The filter boss includes 2 threaded inlet ports and 1 outlet port. We recommend replacement of the first filter at 500 miles and replacement of the second and third filters after 3,000 miles each. After three filter changes, replacement can be done once a year. title: Sinister Diesel 5" DPF-Back Exhaust for 2011-2016 Ford F250/350/450 6.7L
description: Fitment Fits 2011–2016 Ford F-250 / F-350 6.7L Powerstroke (All Pickup Cab & Bed Lengths – Does Not Fit Cab & Chassis) 50-State Legal Key Features Patent Pending Thermal Induction Port Design – Precision-engineered induction ports integrated into the tailpipe section introduce ambient airflow into the exhaust stream, helping reduce tailpipe outlet temperatures by up to 30%. 409 Stainless Steel Construction – Corrosion-resistant 409 stainless steel built for long-term durability. 5-Inch Mandrel-Bent Tubing – Improves exhaust flow from the DPF rearward for better efficiency and deeper tone. All Cab & Bed Length Fitment – Designed to fit all pickup configurations; not compatible with Cab & Chassis models. Direct Bolt-On Installation – Includes all required hardware and gaskets for a straightforward installation. Product Overview Most DPF-back systems on the market are simply larger tubing with a polished tip. They improve sound and appearance — but that’s where the engineering stops. The Sinister Diesel 5" DPF-Back Exhaust System is engineered specifically for 2011–2016 6.7L Powerstroke pickups and delivers both aggressive sound and functional design. Constructed from precision mandrel-bent 409 stainless steel, this system increases exhaust efficiency while maintaining factory emissions components. Our patent pending Thermal Induction Port design is integrated directly into the tailpipe section. As exhaust exits the system, these precision-cut induction ports draw in ambient air and introduce it into the exhaust stream. This engineered airflow mixing effect helps reduce exhaust outlet temperatures by up to 30% at the tailpipe. To ensure proper fitment across all pickup cab and bed lengths, minor trimming may be required depending on your configuration. This adjustment is quick, straightforward, and allows for precise final positioning of the system. Lower outlet temperatures mean less concentrated heat at the bumper, trailer, hitch components, and surrounding areas — giving you more than just sound and appearance. The larger 5-inch tubing produces a deeper, more aggressive exhaust tone without excessive drone, making it ideal for daily driving, towing, or highway cruising. What’s Included 5" DPF-Back Exhaust System Sensor Plug (Some 11-16 Trucks require a sensor in the tail pipe) Sinister Diesel 5" to 6" Rolled Edge Polished Exhaust Tip with Thermal Induction Ports Installation Hardware and Gaskets 1-Year Warranty Against Manufacturer Defects Why Choose Sinister Diesel Many exhaust systems focus solely on diameter and tone. Sinister Diesel engineers additional function into the details. The patent pending Thermal Induction Port design actively helps manage exhaust heat at the outlet — delivering engineered performance, durability, and a finished look that stands apart.

title: Mishimoto Aluminum Primary Radiator for 2017+ Ford Power Stroke 6.7L
description: Mishimoto is excited to expand our line of Ford Powerstroke cooling products to the 2017+ 6.7L Powerstroke with our all-aluminum, direct-fit primary radiator. We've taken our experience with previous generations of Powerstroke cooling products and combined it to create a larger, stronger, and more capable primary radiator for the 2017+ 6.7L Powerstroke. Extruded tubes with internal struts help distribute stress and provide greater strength and pressure tolerance. Our rubberized upper mounting peg design is carried over from our 6.4L and previous generation 6.7L Powerstroke radiators as well. These rubberized pegs help absorb frame flex and redirect it away from the radiator's core. Increased reliability wasn't our only goal for this 6.7L Powerstroke primary radiator, however. The 14% increase in core volume, 68% increase in surface area, and 62% increase in coolant capacity add up to a radiator that's capable of cooling even the most demanding truck. Of course, this 2017+ 6.7L Powerstroke primary radiator is backed by our Mishimoto Lifetime Warranty, so you never have to worry about buying another radiator again.

title: Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration System (W/ WIX) for 2008-2010 Ford Powerstroke 6.4L
description: It doesn't matter if your 6.4L Ford Powerstroke engine is brand new or has had a long service life, there are contaminants like casting sand leftover from the manufacturing process, metal shavings, rust, and mineral deposits floating in your coolant that can diminish the heat capacity of your truck's cooling system. Over time, these dangerous deposits can even cause damage to your water pump bearings and the small passages inside your EGR cooler and oil cooler. The CNC machined billet 6061 aluminum anodized construction filter boss attaches to a supplied bracket, which mounts to factory holes so no drilling is required. This kit includes all fittings needed for installation. The filter can be easily unscrewed for replacement. We recommend replacement of the first filter at 500 miles with replacement of the second and third filters is at 3,000 miles each. After 3 changes, filter replacement can be done once a year. The silicone hoses fittings are pre-assembled for simple, leak-proof connections that factory tested at twice the recommended radiator cap pressure. Features and Benefits: The Filter Block is CNC Machined from Solid Billet 6061 Aluminum High Strength, Abrasion Resistant Silicon Hoses Extends the life of your Water Pump, Oil Cooler, and EGR Cooler Maintains optimal coolant flow as this is a bypass filtration kit 1 Year Warranty Includes: Genuine WIX Coolant Filter Filter Block Mounting Bracket Silicon Hoses Installation Hardware FAQs: Q: What is the installation time for this product? A: The Sinister Diesel Coolant Filer Bypass system takes approximately 2 hours for installation. Installation time may vary based on experience. Q: Why do I need a Coolant Filtration system? A: From the factory, many engine blocks will have sand, from the casting process, still in the cooling channels. If not filtered out, the sand can clog EGR coolers or cause premature wear of water pumps or coolant seals. Our Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration System is a stylish and affordable "insurance" against premature failures from debris in the coolant system.

title: Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration System (W/ CAT) for 2020-2022 Ford Powerstroke 6.7L
description: Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration System – 2020–2022 Ford F-250 / F-350 / F-450 6.7L Powerstroke Key Features • Removes Harmful Contaminants – Helps capture casting sand, metal particles, and debris before they circulate through the cooling system • Helps Protect Critical Cooling Components – Supports longer life for the water pump, EGR cooler, heater core, oil cooler, and cooling system seals • CNC-Machined Billet Filter Head – Precision-machined from 6061 aluminum and anodized in Sinister Blue • Genuine CAT Coolant Filter – Proven filtration designed to remove contaminants without restricting coolant flow • Vehicle-Specific Mounting System – Mounts using factory locations with no drilling required • Reinforced Silicone Hoses – Built to withstand heat, pressure, and long-term use • Pre-Assembled Hose Connections – Designed for straightforward installation and reliable sealing • Serviceable Design – Filter can be easily replaced during routine maintenance • Direct Bolt-On Installation – Includes all required fittings and hardware • 1-Year Warranty – Backed by Sinister Diesel for quality and reliability Product Overview The Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration System for the 2020–2022 6.7L Powerstroke is designed to continuously remove harmful contaminants from your truck's cooling system before they can damage critical components. Even from the factory, engine blocks often contain residual casting sand, metal particles, machining debris, sealant residue, and other contaminants left behind during the manufacturing process. As coolant circulates through the engine, these particles can wear on water pumps, restrict coolant passages, clog heat exchangers, and shorten the lifespan of expensive cooling system components. Our coolant filtration system continuously bypasses and filters a small amount of coolant while the engine is running, gradually removing contaminants from circulation. Cleaner coolant helps support long-term reliability and provides added protection for critical components such as the water pump, EGR cooler, heater core, oil cooler, and cooling system seals. At the heart of the system is a CNC-machined billet aluminum filter head finished in Sinister Blue anodizing and paired with a Genuine CAT coolant filter. The assembly mounts securely using factory mounting locations, eliminating the need for drilling or permanent modifications. The included reinforced silicone hoses feature pre-assembled connections designed for straightforward installation and reliable operation. Every fitting, bracket, and mounting component required for installation is included in the kit. Whether your truck is stock, used for towing, or built for heavy-duty work, coolant filtration is one of the simplest and most effective ways to help protect your cooling system investment for years to come. Recommended Filter Service Schedule The first few filters remove the majority of casting sand, manufacturing debris, and contaminants from the cooling system. • Initial Filter: Replace after 500 miles • Second Filter: Replace after 1,500 miles • Third Filter: Replace after 3,000 miles • Maintenance Interval: Replace at every oil change after that Fitment Note Fits 2020–2022 Ford F-250, F-350, and F-450 equipped with the 6.7L Powerstroke Diesel engine. Compatibility Notice Some aftermarket disaster prevention kits and cooling system modifications may interfere with the installation or operation of this coolant filtration system. Compatibility cannot be guaranteed when used with non-Sinister Diesel components. What’s Included (1) Genuine CAT Coolant Filter (1) CNC-Machined Billet Aluminum Filter Head (1) Vehicle-Specific Mounting Bracket Reinforced Silicone Hoses All Required Installation Hardware Why Choose Sinister Diesel Since 2009, Sinister Diesel has been developing solutions that help diesel owners improve reliability and prevent common failures. Our coolant filtration systems are engineered to remove harmful contaminants from the cooling system, helping protect critical components and providing long-term peace of mind for Powerstroke owners.

description: Sinister Diesel Turbo Drain Tube – 2003–2007 Ford 6.0L Powerstroke Key Features • Improved Oil Drainage – Less restrictive bend allows oil to exit the turbo faster, reducing heat buildup and oil breakdown • Protects Turbocharger – Prevents oil from sitting and overheating inside the turbo, helping extend turbo life • Upgraded Construction – Thicker, high-quality material for increased durability over the factory drain tube • Direct Replacement Fit – Designed to install in place of the OEM drain with no modifications required • O-Rings Included – New seals provided to ensure a leak-free installation Product Overview Ford identified a critical issue with the factory turbo drain design on the 6.0L Powerstroke—the stock drain tube can restrict oil flow, allowing oil to sit inside the turbo too long. title: Sinister Diesel STC HPOP Fitting Kit for 2004-2007 6.0L Powerstroke
description: Features: Most current and up to date "Snap-To-Connect" kit Design specifically for: Ford 6.0L Power Stroke V8 Ford 4.5L Power Stroke V6 International VT-375 International VT-275 The Sinister Diesel 6.0 Powerstroke STC HPOP Fitting Update Kit was the redesign of the high-pressure oil pump for the 2005 and newer Powerstroke Diesel Engines. The "Snap-To-Connect" or STC Fitting used to connect the high-pressure oil pump to the oil supply branch tube has been problematic. The two-piece fittings have a tendency to flex causing wear on the seal and the locking ring resulting in leaks and occasionally the complete separation of the fitting. When a leak occurs at this fitting, the engine may be difficult to start or may not start at all. Depending on the severity of the leak the engine may stall, sometimes abruptly and without warning, creating a dangerous situation for the driver and other motorist. This kit solves the problem by including a bracket designed to support the fitting and branch tube and preventing any movement of the branch tube and eliminating the possibility of the fitting to become separated. Kit Includes: (1) Support Bracket (1) Hydraulic Pump Seal Kit (1) Branch Tube Adapter - Male (1) HPOP Adapter Fitting - Female (2) M6x35mm Branch Tube Bolts (2) M8x60mm Pump Bolts (1) M8x45mm Pump Bolts

description: Sinister Diesel Billet Catch Can Kit – 6.0L Powerstroke Fits 2003–2007 Ford F-250 / F-350 / F-450 / F-550 Super Duty Key Features • Fully machined from billet aluminum for superior strength and durability • High-flow design allows crankcase ventilation while filtering out oil, fuel, and water vapor • Fully baffled internal system separates contaminants effectively for cleaner operation • Serviceable design lets you inspect and drain captured oil without full removal • Can be opened while installed for easy maintenance • Frame rail-mounted for a clean, secure installation • Designed for simple installation using supplied hardware Product Overview Protect your 6.0L Powerstroke from harmful oil blow-by and buildup with the Sinister Diesel Billet Catch Can Kit. Designed to keep your intake, turbo, and intercooler system clean, this high-flow catch can filters out oil mist, water vapor, and fuel residue before they can re-enter the intake system. By preventing oil from coating your charge pipes, intercooler, and intake manifold, the Sinister Diesel Catch Can helps maintain optimal airflow and thermal efficiency—reducing carbon buildup, extending turbo life, and promoting cleaner combustion. Built from precision-machined billet aluminum, the canister is fully baffled inside for maximum separation efficiency. The design allows easy draining and inspection while still installed, making maintenance simple and hassle-free. Mounted securely to the frame rail with the included bracket and hardware, this system offers both durability and convenience. What’s Included • (1) Billet Aluminum Catch Can • Complete Installation Hardware Kit Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) Q: Can I use this kit with an aftermarket bumper? A: Yes, though the can’s location may need to be adjusted slightly. The included hardware allows for flexible mounting in different positions as needed. Q: Will I still need to service my CCV system after installing the Sinister Diesel Billet Catch Can? A: Yes, but far less frequently. The catch can captures most of the oil and blow-by that would normally cycle through the CCV system, keeping it cleaner and reducing buildup. You’ll just need to periodically inspect and drain the catch can. Q: How often should I drain the catch can? A: For most driving conditions, check and drain the can every 3,000–5,000 miles, or sooner if you notice increased oil accumulation. Q: Does this system require removing the factory CCV system? A: No. The Sinister Diesel Catch Can works in line with the factory CCV system, capturing oil and contaminants before they can be recirculated back into the intake. Q: What material is the can made from? A: Each unit is CNC-machined from billet aluminum for exceptional strength, corrosion resistance, and long-term durability.

title: Sinister Diesel Fuel Filter Conversion Kit for 2011-2026 Ford Powerstroke 6.7L
description: Sinister Diesel Fuel Filter Conversion Kit - 6.7L Powerstroke (2011-2026) Fitment Fits 2011-2026 Ford F-250 / F-350 / F-450 / F-550 Super Duty 6.7L Powerstroke Key Features Billet Aluminum Fuel Filter Housing - Permanently replaces the failure-prone factory plastic housing True 2011-2026 Compatibility - Includes integrated third fuel port for 2017+ trucks plus threaded plug for 2011-2016 applications Updated Leak-Resistant Design - Redesigned housing eliminates unnecessary side ports to reduce potential leak points Improved OEM Quick Connect Ports - Redesigned fittings allow easier installation of factory fuel line connections Grooved O-Ring Sealing System - Improved sealing design helps simplify installation while reducing the chance of seepage or leaks Buna O-Ring Locking System - Provides a clean, secure seal into the factory cup without bulky retention sleeves Baldwin 4-Micron Nominal Filter - Matches OEM-level filtration standards while maintaining proper fuel flow CNC-Machined Billet Construction - Built to handle heat, pressure, vibration, and long-term service Direct Bolt-On Installation - No cutting, no fabrication, and no extra adapters required Product Overview The factory 6.7L Powerstroke fuel filter housing is one of the most common leak points on these trucks. Over time, the factory plastic housing becomes prone to cracking, warping, and leaking from repeated heat cycles, vibration, and fuel system service. In many cases, this leads to diesel fuel leaks or fuel spray directly into the engine bay. The Sinister Diesel Fuel Filter Conversion Kit was engineered to permanently eliminate those factory weak points with a precision CNC-machined billet aluminum housing designed for long-term durability and leak-resistant performance. This newest generation housing features several major design improvements focused specifically on reliability and sealing performance. We eliminated the unnecessary side ports found in earlier and competing designs - because fewer ports means fewer potential leak points. The fuel ports themselves were also redesigned to better accommodate the factory Ford quick-connect fuel lines, making installation easier and improving overall fitment. To further improve sealing performance, the housing now uses a grooved O-ring retention design. This helps properly position the seal during installation while adding another layer of protection against seepage and leaks - critical on a product specifically designed to solve factory fuel leaks. To secure the housing, we use a Buna O-ring locking system that provides a clean, secure seal into the factory cup. This eliminates the oversized "can koozie" style retention sleeves seen in other designs while simplifying installation and improving long-term sealing reliability. Unlike competitor kits that rely on extra adapters and multiple connection points to make one housing fit every year range, this system was engineered from the start to properly support both configurations: 2011-2016 trucks use a 2-port system and include a threaded plug 2017-2026 trucks use the integrated 3-port configuration included in the housing No stacked fittings. No unnecessary adapters. No extra leak points. Filtration performance is just as important as housing durability. That's why this kit includes the Baldwin BF7967 fuel filter with 4-micron nominal filtration - closely matching OEM filtration standards while maintaining proper fuel flow for the high-pressure common rail fuel system. Many competing kits use filters rated at 9 microns or larger, allowing significantly more particulate matter to pass through the fuel system. On modern injection systems where tolerances are measured in microns, filtration precision matters. This kit was designed to improve reliability without compromising fuel system protection. Common Problems This Solves Cracked factory plastic fuel filter housing Fuel leaks at the filter housing or cap Diesel fuel spray in the engine bay Weak factory plastic construction Leak-prone multi-adapter competitor kits Difficult-to-service retention sleeve systems Service Interval Follow OEM fuel filter replacement intervals. Most trucks require service approximately every 15,000 miles or annually, depending on fuel quality and driving conditions. What's Included (1) CNC-Machined Billet Aluminum Fuel Filter Housing (1) Baldwin BF7967 4-Micron Nominal Fuel Filter Integrated 3rd Port with Threaded Plug for 2011-2016 Applications Billet Aluminum Fuel Fittings Installation Hardware Why Choose Sinister Diesel Since 2009, Sinister Diesel has been building parts designed to solve factory weak points with stronger materials, smarter engineering, and real-world reliability. Our 6.7L Powerstroke Fuel Filter Conversion Kit has become one of the most trusted upgrades in the diesel industry because it fixes a known OEM problem with a permanent billet aluminum solution designed to last.

New O-Rings & Retaining Clips Included – No need to reuse fatigued factory components. Product Overview The 2023+ 6.7L Powerstroke High Output engine operates at higher factory boost levels, increasing the demand on the charge air system. From the factory, the cold-side pipe is plastic and the hot-side pipe is a restrictive 2.5" metal tube. Under increased boost pressure, these components can expand, fatigue, or become airflow bottlenecks — especially under towing or heavy load. Sinister Diesel replaces both sides with full 3" mandrel-bent, TIG-welded aluminum tubing engineered for strength and airflow efficiency. The hot-side pipe is designed specifically for the High Output platform and includes the proper temperature sensor provision for seamless OEM integration. Independent airflow testing confirms measurable improvements: Up to 61% less restriction on the hot side compared to factory piping Up to 27% less restriction on the cold side across the usable airflow range Lower restriction means the turbo does not have to work as hard to move the same volume of air — improving overall charge system efficiency under load. The factory cold-side pipe is plastic. Some aftermarket kits use minimal aluminum and heavily rely on silicone or rubber instead of full aluminum piping. Often only a short section is upgraded while long silicone sections are used because they are less expensive and easier to produce than mandrel-bent, TIG-welded, powder-coated aluminum tubing. Excessive silicone or rubber can expand and billow under heavy boost, disrupting clean airflow and reducing structural stability. Aluminum maintains its shape under pressure, preserving consistent airflow characteristics under higher boost levels found in the High Output platform. Many competing kits also require you to remove and reuse factory O-rings and retaining clips from your original charge pipes. These components are often heat-cycled and fatigued. Sinister Diesel includes new O-rings and retaining clips for both hot and cold side connections — ensuring a proper seal and eliminating the risk of reinstalling worn sealing components. To further prevent boost leaks, our silicone boots feature a molded No-Slip Lip — an internal ridge that mechanically locks onto the pipe bead. Combined with our 3/4" wide stainless steel T-bolt clamps, this design helps prevent boot slippage under heavy boost or tuned conditions. This is not a cosmetic upgrade. It is a structural, airflow, and reliability improvement built to support the increased demands of the 6.7L High Output engine. A: The factory plastic charge pipe is prone to cracking and boost leaks over time. Upgrading to an aluminum charge pipe provides increased strength, improved heat resistance, and more consistent boost pressure delivery—reducing the risk of unexpected failures. Will this fit my truck? A: This kit fits 2023-2026 Ford Super Duty trucks (F-250 through F-550) equipped with the 6.7L HO Power Stroke diesel engine and factory turbo configuration. Verify model year and engine before ordering. Is installation difficult? A: Installation is straightforward for most DIY mechanics and typically takes 1–2 hours using basic hand tools. description: Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration System – 2019–2024 Ram 2500/3500 6.7 Cummins Diesel Why You Need This Kit: Protects your heater core — the most common coolant failure point on Cummins trucks Filters out casting sand, metal shavings, and debris that the factory system can’t catch Keeps coolant passages clean, extending the life of your water pump, oil cooler, and EGR cooler Prevents costly breakdowns by stopping contamination before it reaches critical components Easy upkeep — filters are quick to replace with simple, tool-free service FREE SHIPPING Description: The Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration Kit is the best insurance you can buy for your 6.7L Cummins. The factory cooling system allows casting sand, rust, and metal debris to circulate, which clogs the heater core and shortens the life of vital parts like the water pump, oil cooler, and EGR cooler. Our kit solves this by continuously filtering small amounts of coolant while the engine runs, trapping harmful contaminants before they do damage. Engineered from CNC-machined billet 6061 aluminum and finished in signature Sinister Gray, this system is built to last. It mounts directly to factory holes (no drilling required) and comes with reinforced silicone hoses that are pre-assembled for leak-proof connections tested to twice the normal system pressure. Maintenance is simple: replace the first filter at 500 miles, the second at 1,500 miles, and then every oil change interval thereafter. What’s Included: Genuine WIX Coolant Filter Sinister Diesel Billet Filter Block Mounting Bracket Reinforced Silicone Hoses (pre-assembled) Installation Hardware Features: CNC-Machined 6061 Aluminum, anodized in Sinister Gray Cleaner coolant = longer engine life Leak-proof, reinforced silicone hoses 1-Year Warranty Coolant Filter Q&A for Sinister Diesel 2019–2024 6.7L Cummins Coolant Filtration Kit What filter does this kit use? The Sinister Diesel 6.7 Cummins Coolant Filtration Kit includes a WIX 24070 spin-on coolant filter. It traps casting sand, rust, and metal shavings that cause heater core, EGR cooler, and oil cooler failures. Why a bypass filter instead of inline? The system filters a small percentage of coolant each time the engine runs until all coolant has been cleaned. An inline filter could block flow if it ever clogged, but our bypass design has a built-in fail-safe—coolant still circulates normally with zero risk to the engine or components. Why WIX? It offers the perfect balance of filtration and flow for the Cummins 6.7L system. The compact size fits our bracket perfectly, and the 27-micron media removes fine debris without affecting coolant pressure. Does it change coolant chemistry? No. The WIX 24070 is a non-chemical filter—no pellets or additives. It won’t alter your coolant blend or require SCA top-offs. How often should I replace the filter? • First change: 500 miles • Second: around 1,000 miles • Third: at 3,000 miles • After that: every oil filter service This schedule keeps your cooling system clean and prevents buildup in small coolant passages. Will it restrict coolant flow? No. The filter is engineered for high flow and low restriction, so your coolant pressure stays stable while debris is trapped safely inside the element. How does this protect my Cummins engine? By constantly cleaning the coolant, the system prevents abrasive debris from circulating and wearing out your water pump, heater core, or coolers—saving you from costly repairs. Quick Takeaway • Kit is designed specifically for Ram 2500/3500 Cummins • 27-micron filtration for debris control • Safe bypass design—no coolant blockage risk • Maintenance made easy with common WIX replacements • Extends cooling-system life and reliability

title: Enshield Protection
ARP Fastener Assembly Lubricant (100-9910)
description: ARP Ultra Torque Fastener Assembly Lubricant Because of frictional inconsistencies with oil, moly, and other lubricants, it takes multiple cycles of torquing the bolt, loosening it, and tightening it again to obtain the required preload or stretch. Typically, when a fastener is first tightened, friction is at its highest point and the preload value or stretch is low. As the fastener is cycled numerous times the friction decreases and the preload increases. This phenomenon is called "preload scatter." With ARP Ultra Torque assembly lubricant, a fastener need only be torqued once to get within 5 percent of ideal preload, and it remains consistent through all subsequent cycles (from race-prep machining through final assembly). Moreover, given the inconsistencies of ordinary lubes, it's quite possible for adjacent head bolts or studs to have vastly different preloads. This scatter causes bore distortion, hampers piston ring seal, and leads to poor head gasket sealing. Order the tube or container size needed for your applications.

Sinister Diesel Billet Catch Can Kit – 6.7L Powerstroke Fits 2017–2023 Ford F-250 / F-350 / F-450 / F-550 Super Duty Key Features • Fully machined from billet aluminum for unmatched strength and durability • High-flow design lets your crankcase breathe freely while filtering oil, fuel, and water vapor • Fully baffled internals maximize separation efficiency for cleaner airflow • Serviceable can allows you to easily check and drain captured oil buildup • Can be opened while installed for fast inspection or cleaning • Frame rail-mounted for a clean, factory-style installation • Designed for simple, straightforward installation with included hardware Product Overview Protect your 6.7L Powerstroke from oil contamination and buildup with the Sinister Diesel Billet Catch Can Kit. This high-quality system captures oil vapor, unburned fuel, and moisture from the crankcase ventilation (CCV) system before they re-enter the intake. By keeping this blow-by out of your turbo, intercooler, and intake manifold, it helps maintain power, reliability, and engine longevity. Oil residue inside the turbo can cause imbalance and premature wear, while deposits in the intercooler reduce cooling efficiency. The Sinister Diesel Catch Can prevents these issues by filtering contaminants and ensuring only clean air re-enters your intake system. Constructed from CNC-machined billet aluminum with fully baffled internals, this catch can is built to last. It mounts securely to the frame rail and can be drained or cleaned without removal. The result is better protection, longer component life, and peace of mind every time you drive. What’s Included • (1) Billet Aluminum Catch Can • Complete Installation Hardware Kit Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) Q: Can I use this kit with an aftermarket bumper? A: Yes. Depending on your bumper setup, the catch can may need to be relocated slightly. The included hardware allows flexible mounting options to fit your configuration. Q: Does this system require removing the factory CCV system? A: No. The Sinister Diesel Catch Can works in line with the factory CCV system, capturing contaminants before they can circulate back through the intake. Q: How often should I drain the catch can? A: We recommend checking and draining it every 3,000–5,000 miles, or more often under heavy towing or performance driving. Q: Do I need tuning or modifications for this kit? A: No. The catch can is a 100% bolt-on system that installs easily with basic hand tools—no tuning or permanent modifications required. Q: Will this mount up without any modifications? A: The system is designed to fit most stock applications without modification. However, vehicles equipped with lift kits, aftermarket accessories, or suspension upgrades may require minor adjustments for optimal fitment. The included bracket offers flexibility, and in most cases, a simple adjustment or additional mounting hole is all that’s needed for a clean, secure install. Q: What material is the catch can made from? A: Each can is CNC-machined from solid billet aluminum, offering excellent durability and corrosion resistance. Q: Will this affect drivability or emissions? A: No. The catch can operates within the factory crankcase ventilation system and does not interfere with emissions components or tuning.

Product Overview The factory cold-side charge pipe on the 2017–2026 6.7L Powerstroke is constructed from a combination of plastic and rubber sections. While designed for cost efficiency and flexibility, these materials expand under boost pressure and repeated heat cycles. It is common for the factory plastic connector near the throttle valve to crack, or for the rubber section to split over time — creating a massive boost leak and sudden loss of power. Rubber expands. Plastic fatigues. Aluminum maintains its shape. Under towing, heavy load, or tuned conditions, expansion in the factory plastic and rubber piping can disrupt airflow consistency and increase the likelihood of boost leaks. Sinister Diesel replaces the factory assembly with a full 3" mandrel-bent, TIG-welded aluminum charge pipe engineered for structural strength and airflow efficiency. Independent airflow testing confirms up to 27% less restriction compared to factory piping across the usable airflow range. Lower restriction improves overall charge system efficiency and helps maintain consistent boost delivery under load. Why 3" Instead of 3.5"? Some aftermarket options advertise oversized 3.5" tubing. While larger diameter may sound appealing, airflow performance is not determined by size alone. Charge air systems must balance diameter, air velocity, and turbo response. Oversizing tubing beyond what the turbo and intercooler can efficiently support can reduce air velocity and increase system volume without delivering measurable gains. Sinister Diesel engineered this cold-side pipe at 3" to optimize airflow efficiency, maintain proper air velocity, and support real-world towing and tuned applications — without sacrificing boost response. This diameter provides substantial airflow improvement over the restrictive factory plastic and rubber assembly while preserving system balance and drivability. To further prevent boost leaks, our silicone boot features a molded No-Slip Lip — an internal ridge that mechanically locks onto the pipe bead. Combined with our 3/4" wide stainless steel T-bolt clamps, this design helps prevent boot slippage under heavy boost or tuned applications. Unlike many competitors, Sinister Diesel includes new O-rings and retaining clips. Many upgrades require reusing factory sealing components that have been heat-cycled and fatigued. This is not a cosmetic upgrade. It is a structural and airflow improvement designed to eliminate weak points in the charge system. No-Slip Lip Silicone Boot – Internal ridge locks onto the pipe bead to help prevent boost blow-off. 5-Ply High-Performance Silicone Boot – Designed for durability under elevated boost pressure. 3/4" Wide Stainless Steel T-Bolt Clamps – Heavy-duty clamping force for secure sealing. Product Overview The factory cold-side charge pipe on the 2017–2026 6.7L Powerstroke is constructed from a combination of plastic and rubber sections. While designed for cost efficiency and flexibility, these materials expand under boost pressure and repeated heat cycles. It is common for the factory plastic connector near the throttle valve to crack, or for the rubber section to split over time — creating a massive boost leak and sudden loss of power. Rubber expands. Plastic fatigues. Aluminum maintains its shape. Under towing, heavy load, or tuned conditions, expansion in the factory plastic and rubber piping can disrupt airflow consistency and increase the likelihood of boost leaks. Sinister Diesel replaces the factory assembly with a full 3" mandrel-bent, TIG-welded aluminum charge pipe engineered for structural strength and airflow efficiency. Independent airflow testing confirms up to 27% less restriction compared to factory piping across the usable airflow range. Lower restriction improves overall charge system efficiency and helps maintain consistent boost delivery under load. Why 3" Instead of 3.5"? Some aftermarket options advertise oversized 3.5" tubing. While larger diameter may sound appealing, airflow performance is not determined by size alone. Charge air systems must balance diameter, air velocity, and turbo response. Oversizing tubing beyond what the turbo and intercooler can efficiently support can reduce air velocity and increase system volume without delivering measurable gains. Sinister Diesel engineered this cold-side pipe at 3" to optimize airflow efficiency, maintain proper air velocity, and support real-world towing and tuned applications — without sacrificing boost response. This diameter provides substantial airflow improvement over the restrictive factory plastic and rubber assembly while preserving system balance and drivability. To further prevent boost leaks, our silicone boot features a molded No-Slip Lip — an internal ridge that mechanically locks onto the pipe bead. Combined with our 3/4" wide stainless steel T-bolt clamps, this design helps prevent boot slippage under heavy boost or tuned applications. Unlike many competitors, Sinister Diesel includes new O-rings and retaining clips. Many upgrades require reusing factory sealing components that have been heat-cycled and fatigued. This is not a cosmetic upgrade. It is a structural and airflow improvement designed to eliminate weak points in the charge system. Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration System – 2020–2022 Ford F-250 / F-350 / F-450 6.7L Powerstroke Key Features • Removes Harmful Contaminants – Helps capture casting sand, metal particles, and debris before they circulate through the cooling system • Helps Protect Critical Cooling Components – Supports longer life for the water pump, EGR cooler, heater core, oil cooler, and cooling system seals • CNC-Machined Billet Filter Head – Precision-machined from 6061 aluminum and anodized in Sinister Blue • Genuine CAT Coolant Filter – Proven filtration designed to remove contaminants without restricting coolant flow • Vehicle-Specific Mounting System – Mounts using factory locations with no drilling required • Reinforced Silicone Hoses – Built to withstand heat, pressure, and long-term use • Pre-Assembled Hose Connections – Designed for straightforward installation and reliable sealing • Serviceable Design – Filter can be easily replaced during routine maintenance • Direct Bolt-On Installation – Includes all required fittings and hardware • 1-Year Warranty – Backed by Sinister Diesel for quality and reliability Product Overview The Sinister Diesel Coolant Filtration System for the 2020–2022 6.7L Powerstroke is designed to continuously remove harmful contaminants from your truck's cooling system before they can damage critical components. Even from the factory, engine blocks often contain residual casting sand, metal particles, machining debris, sealant residue, and other contaminants left behind during the manufacturing process. As coolant circulates through the engine, these particles can wear on water pumps, restrict coolant passages, clog heat exchangers, and shorten the lifespan of expensive cooling system components. Our coolant filtration system continuously bypasses and filters a small amount of coolant while the engine is running, gradually removing contaminants from circulation. Cleaner coolant helps support long-term reliability and provides added protection for critical components such as the water pump, EGR cooler, heater core, oil cooler, and cooling system seals. At the heart of the system is a CNC-machined billet aluminum filter head finished in Sinister Blue anodizing and paired with a Genuine CAT coolant filter. The assembly mounts securely using factory mounting locations, eliminating the need for drilling or permanent modifications. The included reinforced silicone hoses feature pre-assembled connections designed for straightforward installation and reliable operation. Every fitting, bracket, and mounting component required for installation is included in the kit. Whether your truck is stock, used for towing, or built for heavy-duty work, coolant filtration is one of the simplest and most effective ways to help protect your cooling system investment for years to come. Recommended Filter Service Schedule The first few filters remove the majority of casting sand, manufacturing debris, and contaminants from the cooling system. • Initial Filter: Replace after 500 miles • Second Filter: Replace after 1,500 miles • Third Filter: Replace after 3,000 miles • Maintenance Interval: Replace at every oil change after that Fitment Note Fits 2020–2022 Ford F-250, F-350, and F-450 equipped with the 6.7L Powerstroke Diesel engine. Compatibility Notice Some aftermarket disaster prevention kits and cooling system modifications may interfere with the installation or operation of this coolant filtration system. Compatibility cannot be guaranteed when used with non-Sinister Diesel components. Our coolant filtration systems are engineered to remove harmful contaminants from the cooling system, helping protect critical components and providing long-term peace of mind for Powerstroke owners.

From the factory, the 6.0L and 6.4L PowerStroke engine (found in the 2003 to 2010 Ford Super Duty) had a plastic oil filter cap, which becomes weak and brittle over time. And it looks good while getting the job done, too! This Ford 6.0/6.4 oil filter cap has a tough, heat-resistant design constructed of billet aluminum with a Sinister Diesel's signature blue anodized finish. description: Sale Price: $369.99 Clearance Price: $277.99 All sales are final for clearance items. We do our best to show in the pictures any blemishes, scratches or dents, all blemishes are purely aesthetic and do not affects the performance or reliability of the part. CARB EO # D-721-8 Currently ONLY valid for 2017-2019 Ford F250 F350 6.7L Powerstroke 50 State Legal. More year ranges are in the works with CARB Fitment Fits 2017–2026 Ford F-250 / F-350 / F-450 / F-550 6.7L Powerstroke (Standard & High Output) Note: Compatible with 2017–2026 F-250, F-350, and F-450 Pickup models. Compatible with 2017–2022 F-450 and F-550 Cab & Chassis models. Not compatible with 2023+ F-450 or F-550 Cab & Chassis models. Key Features 3" Mandrel-Bent Aluminum Tubing – Replaces factory plastic and rubber piping with rigid, high-strength aluminum. Up to 27% Less Restriction vs. Stock – Verified through independent airflow testing. No-Slip Lip Silicone Boot – Internal ridge locks onto the pipe bead to help prevent boost blow-off. 5-Ply High-Performance Silicone Boot – Designed for durability under elevated boost pressure. 3/4" Wide Stainless Steel T-Bolt Clamps – Heavy-duty clamping force for secure sealing. New O-Rings & Retaining Clips Included – No need to reuse fatigued factory components. TIG-Welded Aluminum Construction – Built for long-term durability and structural integrity. 2-Year Warranty – Backed by Sinister Diesel quality and support. Product Overview The factory cold-side charge pipe on the 2017–2026 6.7L Powerstroke is constructed from a combination of plastic and rubber sections. While designed for cost efficiency and flexibility, these materials expand under boost pressure and repeated heat cycles. It is common for the factory plastic connector near the throttle valve to crack, or for the rubber section to split over time — creating a massive boost leak and sudden loss of power. Rubber expands. Plastic fatigues. Aluminum maintains its shape. Under towing, heavy load, or tuned conditions, expansion in the factory plastic and rubber piping can disrupt airflow consistency and increase the likelihood of boost leaks. Sinister Diesel replaces the factory assembly with a full 3" mandrel-bent, TIG-welded aluminum charge pipe engineered for structural strength and airflow efficiency. Independent airflow testing confirms up to 27% less restriction compared to factory piping across the usable airflow range. Lower restriction improves overall charge system efficiency and helps maintain consistent boost delivery under load. Why 3" Instead of 3.5"? Some aftermarket options advertise oversized 3.5" tubing. While larger diameter may sound appealing, airflow performance is not determined by size alone. Charge air systems must balance diameter, air velocity, and turbo response. Oversizing tubing beyond what the turbo and intercooler can efficiently support can reduce air velocity and increase system volume without delivering measurable gains. Sinister Diesel engineered this cold-side pipe at 3" to optimize airflow efficiency, maintain proper air velocity, and support real-world towing and tuned applications — without sacrificing boost response. This diameter provides substantial airflow improvement over the restrictive factory plastic and rubber assembly while preserving system balance and drivability. To further prevent boost leaks, our silicone boot features a molded No-Slip Lip — an internal ridge that mechanically locks onto the pipe bead. Combined with our 3/4" wide stainless steel T-bolt clamps, this design helps prevent boot slippage under heavy boost or tuned applications. Unlike many competitors, Sinister Diesel includes new O-rings and retaining clips. Many upgrades require reusing factory sealing components that have been heat-cycled and fatigued. This is not a cosmetic upgrade. It is a structural and airflow improvement designed to eliminate weak points in the charge system. What’s Included 3" Aluminum Cold-Side Charge Pipe 5-Ply Silicone Boot with No-Slip Lip 3/4" Wide Stainless Steel T-Bolt Clamps New O-Rings New Retaining Clips Required Hardware Installation Instructions Why Choose Sinister Diesel Since 2009, Sinister Diesel has focused on eliminating known diesel failure points through real-world testing and proven engineering. Our products are designed as long-term solutions—not temporary fixes—so you can drive with confidence knowing your truck is protected.

Key Features • CNC-machined from solid T6-6061 billet aluminum for superior strength • Anodized in Limited Edition Sinister Green for durability and custom appearance • Laser-etched Sinister Diesel logo • Tight-sealing Viton O-ring prevents dirt, dust, and moisture intrusion • Integrated magnet in the base allows secure storage on the fuel door or body panel while fueling • Direct replacement for the flimsy factory plastic filler-neck plug • 2-year warranty Product Overview The Sinister Diesel Billet Fuel Plug/Cap is the perfect upgrade for 2013+ Ram Cummins owners who want better protection for their fuel system. Sinister Diesel Fuel Filter Conversion Kit - 6.7L Powerstroke (2011-2026) Fitment Fits 2011-2026 Ford F-250 / F-350 / F-450 / F-550 Super Duty 6.7L Powerstroke Key Features Billet Aluminum Fuel Filter Housing - Permanently replaces the failure-prone factory plastic housing True 2011-2026 Compatibility - Includes integrated third fuel port for 2017+ trucks plus threaded plug for 2011-2016 applications Updated Leak-Resistant Design - Redesigned housing eliminates unnecessary side ports to reduce potential leak points Improved OEM Quick Connect Ports - Redesigned fittings allow easier installation of factory fuel line connections Grooved O-Ring Sealing System - Improved sealing design helps simplify installation while reducing the chance of seepage or leaks Buna O-Ring Locking System - Provides a clean, secure seal into the factory cup without bulky retention sleeves Baldwin 4-Micron Nominal Filter - Matches OEM-level filtration standards while maintaining proper fuel flow CNC-Machined Billet Construction - Built to handle heat, pressure, vibration, and long-term service Direct Bolt-On Installation - No cutting, no fabrication, and no extra adapters required Product Overview The factory 6.7L Powerstroke fuel filter housing is one of the most common leak points on these trucks. Over time, the factory plastic housing becomes prone to cracking, warping, and leaking from repeated heat cycles, vibration, and fuel system service. In many cases, this leads to diesel fuel leaks or fuel spray directly into the engine bay. The Sinister Diesel Fuel Filter Conversion Kit was engineered to permanently eliminate those factory weak points with a precision CNC-machined billet aluminum housing designed for long-term durability and leak-resistant performance. This newest generation housing features several major design improvements focused specifically on reliability and sealing performance. We eliminated the unnecessary side ports found in earlier and competing designs - because fewer ports means fewer potential leak points. The fuel ports themselves were also redesigned to better accommodate the factory Ford quick-connect fuel lines, making installation easier and improving overall fitment. To further improve sealing performance, the housing now uses a grooved O-ring retention design. This helps properly position the seal during installation while adding another layer of protection against seepage and leaks - critical on a product specifically designed to solve factory fuel leaks. To secure the housing, we use a Buna O-ring locking system that provides a clean, secure seal into the factory cup. This eliminates the oversized "can koozie" style retention sleeves seen in other designs while simplifying installation and improving long-term sealing reliability. Unlike competitor kits that rely on extra adapters and multiple connection points to make one housing fit every year range, this system was engineered from the start to properly support both configurations: 2011-2016 trucks use a 2-port system and include a threaded plug 2017-2026 trucks use the integrated 3-port configuration included in the housing No stacked fittings. No unnecessary adapters. No extra leak points. Filtration performance is just as important as housing durability. That's why this kit includes the Baldwin BF7967 fuel filter with 4-micron nominal filtration - closely matching OEM filtration standards while maintaining proper fuel flow for the high-pressure common rail fuel system. Many competing kits use filters rated at 9 microns or larger, allowing significantly more particulate matter to pass through the fuel system. On modern injection systems where tolerances are measured in microns, filtration precision matters. This kit was designed to improve reliability without compromising fuel system protection. Common Problems This Solves Cracked factory plastic fuel filter housing Fuel leaks at the filter housing or cap Diesel fuel spray in the engine bay Weak factory plastic construction Leak-prone multi-adapter competitor kits Difficult-to-service retention sleeve systems Service Interval Follow OEM fuel filter replacement intervals. Most trucks require service approximately every 15,000 miles or annually, depending on fuel quality and driving conditions. Over time, these dangerous deposits can even cause damage to your water pump bearings and the small passages inside your EGR cooler and oil cooler. The CNC machined billet 6061 aluminum anodized construction filter boss attaches to a supplied bracket, which mounts to factory holes so no drilling is required. This kit includes all fittings needed for installation. The filter can be easily unscrewed for replacement. We recommend replacement of the first filter at 500 miles with replacement of the second and third filters is at 3,000 miles each. After 3 changes, filter replacement can be done once a year. The silicone hoses fittings are pre-assembled for simple, leak-proof connections that factory tested at twice the recommended radiator cap pressure. Includes: Genuine WIX Coolant Filter Filter Block Mounting Bracket Nickel plated ball valves Silicone Hoses Molded Silicone T-Hose Installation Hardware Q: What coolant filters work best with Sinister Diesel coolant filtration kits? A: The two most popular and proven options are the WIX 24070 and the CAT 435-5142. Both deliver excellent protection against casting sand, rust, and debris that can clog your heater core, EGR cooler, Oil Cooler or water pump. Q: What’s the difference between the WIX 24070 and the CAT 435-5142? A: The WIX 24070 is slightly taller at 5.25 inches, while the CAT 435-5142 is more compact at 4⅜ inches. The WIX filter is lighter (about 1 lb) and designed for smooth, high-flow operation, while the CAT filter is heavier (about 2 lb) with a thicker steel housing for extreme durability. Q: Which filter do most customers use? A: The WIX 24070 is our standard recommendation for most Sinister Diesel coolant filtration kits. It’s affordable, widely available, and requires no additives—just pure debris filtration and easy maintenance. Q: When should I consider using the CAT 435-5142 instead? A: The CAT 435-5142 is a great upgrade for heavy-duty or high-vibration applications. If your truck tows frequently, runs in harsh conditions, or you prefer OEM-grade construction, the CAT filter’s heavier shell and reinforced core are a solid choice. Q: Does either filter add coolant additives or chemicals? A: No—both filters are non-pelleted. That means they don’t release Supplemental Coolant Additives (SCAs). If you’re running Extended Life Coolant (ELC), that’s perfect—no extra maintenance needed. Q: Are both filters compatible with Sinister Diesel kits? A: Absolutely. Both the WIX 24070 and CAT 435-5142 thread directly into Sinister Diesel coolant filtration heads (11/16-16 thread). Fitment is perfect on Powerstroke, Cummins, and Duramax applications. Q: Which one is better for a limited space under the hood? A: The CAT 435-5142 is shorter at 4⅜", so it’s ideal if your installation area has less vertical clearance. (Which is why it's the only choice for some kits) Q: Which filter provides better coolant flow? A: The WIX 24070 typically offers slightly higher flow due to its taller design and lighter internal construction, making it ideal for daily drivers. Q: How often should these filters be replaced? A: Replace the first filter after 500 miles to remove initial debris from the system. Then replace the second filter around 1,000 miles, and the third one at 3,000 miles. After that, change the coolant filter with every oil filter service to keep your cooling system performing at its best. These intervals apply to both the WIX 24070 and CAT 435-5142. Q: What’s the bottom line better filter? A: WIX 24070: Best all-around filter for most trucks — easy, reliable, and affordable.
