Compatible with 2017–2022 F-450 and F-550 Cab & Chassis models. Not compatible with 2023+ F-450 or F-550 Cab & Chassis models. Key Features 3" Mandrel-Bent Aluminum Tubing – Replaces factory plastic and rubber piping with rigid, high-strength aluminum. Up to 27% Less Restriction vs. Stock – Verified through independent airflow testing. No-Slip Lip Silicone Boot – Internal ridge locks onto the pipe bead to help prevent boost blow-off. 5-Ply High-Performance Silicone Boot – Designed for durability under elevated boost pressure. 3/4" Wide Stainless Steel T-Bolt Clamps – Heavy-duty clamping force for secure sealing. New O-Rings & Retaining Clips Included – No need to reuse fatigued factory components. TIG-Welded Aluminum Construction – Built for long-term durability and structural integrity. 2-Year Warranty – Backed by Sinister Diesel quality and support. Product Overview The factory cold-side charge pipe on the 2017–2026 6.7L Powerstroke is constructed from a combination of plastic and rubber sections. While designed for cost efficiency and flexibility, these materials expand under boost pressure and repeated heat cycles. It is common for the factory plastic connector near the throttle valve to crack, or for the rubber section to split over time — creating a massive boost leak and sudden loss of power. Rubber expands. Plastic fatigues. Aluminum maintains its shape. Under towing, heavy load, or tuned conditions, expansion in the factory plastic and rubber piping can disrupt airflow consistency and increase the likelihood of boost leaks. Sinister Diesel replaces the factory assembly with a full 3" mandrel-bent, TIG-welded aluminum charge pipe engineered for structural strength and airflow efficiency. Independent airflow testing confirms up to 27% less restriction compared to factory piping across the usable airflow range. Lower restriction improves overall charge system efficiency and helps maintain consistent boost delivery under load. Why 3" Instead of 3.5"? Some aftermarket options advertise oversized 3.5" tubing. While larger diameter may sound appealing, airflow performance is not determined by size alone. Charge air systems must balance diameter, air velocity, and turbo response. Oversizing tubing beyond what the turbo and intercooler can efficiently support can reduce air velocity and increase system volume without delivering measurable gains. Sinister Diesel engineered this cold-side pipe at 3" to optimize airflow efficiency, maintain proper air velocity, and support real-world towing and tuned applications — without sacrificing boost response. This diameter provides substantial airflow improvement over the restrictive factory plastic and rubber assembly while preserving system balance and drivability. To further prevent boost leaks, our silicone boot features a molded No-Slip Lip — an internal ridge that mechanically locks onto the pipe bead. Combined with our 3/4" wide stainless steel T-bolt clamps, this design helps prevent boot slippage under heavy boost or tuned applications. Unlike many competitors, Sinister Diesel includes new O-rings and retaining clips. Many upgrades require reusing factory sealing components that have been heat-cycled and fatigued. This is not a cosmetic upgrade. It is a structural and airflow improvement designed to eliminate weak points in the charge system. What’s Included 3" Aluminum Cold-Side Charge Pipe 5-Ply Silicone Boot with No-Slip Lip 3/4" Wide Stainless Steel T-Bolt Clamps New O-Rings New Retaining Clips Required Hardware Installation Instructions Why Choose Sinister Diesel Since 2009, Sinister Diesel has focused on eliminating known diesel failure points through real-world testing and proven engineering. Our products are designed as long-term solutions—not temporary fixes—so you can drive with confidence knowing your truck is protected.

Key Features 3" Mandrel-Bent Aluminum Tubing – Larger than the factory 2.5" pipe to reduce restriction and increase airflow capacity. Up to 61% Less Restriction vs. Stock – Verified through independent airflow testing. TIG-Welded Aluminum Construction – Built for strength and long-term durability under boost. 3/4" Wide Stainless Steel T-Bolt Clamp – Heavy-duty clamping force for secure sealing under load. New O-Ring & Retaining Clip Included – Eliminates the need to reuse fatigued factory components. 2-Year Warranty – Backed by Sinister Diesel quality and support. Product Overview The factory hot-side charge pipe on the 6.7L Powerstroke measures only 2.5 inches in diameter, creating a restriction point as airflow demand increases. Under towing, heavy load, or tuned conditions, this bottleneck forces the turbo to work harder to move the same volume of air. Sinister Diesel upgrades the system to a full 3" mandrel-bent, TIG-welded aluminum pipe engineered to reduce restriction and improve charge system efficiency. Independent airflow testing confirms up to 61% less restriction compared to factory piping. Lower restriction means the turbo does not have to work as hard to reach target boost — improving overall system efficiency and allowing the truck to scale with increased airflow demand. This is not a cosmetic upgrade. It is a measurable airflow improvement. More aluminum means more strength. Some aftermarket options rely heavily on silicone sections instead of full aluminum construction. Silicone can expand under boost and affect airflow consistency. Aluminum maintains its shape under pressure, preserving consistent airflow characteristics and improving structural integrity. Unlike many competitors, Sinister Diesel includes a new O-ring and retaining clip. Many single-pipe upgrades require reusing factory sealing components that have been heat-cycled and fatigued, increasing the risk of boost leaks.
